hook_text_english: A Reddit user sequenced his own DNA at home using Claude.
full_text_english: A Reddit user just read his own DNA at home.No lab. No biology degree. Just a laptop, a card-sized device, and Claude as his tutor. Step by step.Question by question. Three days later, his complete DNA was sitting on his screen.99.8 percent accurate.In India a full DNA test costs up to 1.5 lakh and the lab keeps your data.This man owns every single letter himself.I share updates like these in my WhatsApp community. Link in bio.

hook_text_english: How do I actually become an AI person? Framework shared: PROMPTS.
full_text_english: Did a session at IIM Ahmedabad last week.Room was packed. And every single one of them kept asking the same question."How do I actually become an AI person?"So I gave them a framework. I call it PROMPTS.Sharing it here because a few of you probably need it too.P. Pick your toolkitStop playing with 80 AI tools. Build a stack of 5 to 6 that actually solve your everyday problems.R. Refine.Go deep. Understand temperature settings, max tokens, how different models actually work. That 1% tweak in your prompt changes everything.O. Open your world to AI.Connect your Gmail, Slack, Tally, everything. AI doesn't live inside ChatGPT. It lives everywhere, if you let it.M. Make multiple replicas of yourself.Build a content autopilot. An AI that works while you sleep.P. PipelineBuild agents that run 24/7. Not you doing AI tasks. AI doing your tasks.T. Tinker.Stop waiting for the perfect product. Build it yourself. Vibe code it. No code required.S. Synthesize.This is where the magic actually happens. You stop using 6 different tools. You start orchestrating a team of AI agents that work together.Here's the thing.The students who win in 5 years won't be the ones who "know AI."They'll be the ones who converted their skill into a system using AISkill. System. Business.Two things are inevitable from here.One. The rise of 10M, 100M, even 1B dollar single person companies.Two. The rise of insanely high paid employees inside companies.But both of these futures belong to the same kind of person.Someone who uses AI to compound their own output. Someone who becomes T shaped. AI first. A real problem solver across functions.We like to call them AI Generalists.That's the unfair advantage sitting right in front of you. Most people don't see it.Use it.Which step of PROMPTS are you actually stuck on?Also. Should I record a proper YouTube video walking through PROMPTS in detail? Or just drop the full recording of the IIM A talk as is?Reply and let me know, I'll do whichever gets more votes.
body_summary_english: Based on IIM Ahmedabad session, creator shares a PROMPTS framework for becoming an AI expert: Pick toolkit, Refine skills, Open integrations, and more steps.
comments: The PROMPTS framework just reframed everything for me.I'm an AI content writer, and I realized I was stuck at P — playing with too many tools instead of building a real stack.The shift from "using AI" to "orchestrating AI" is the actual unlock. Most people never make that jump.Skill → System → Business. This is the sequence nobody talks about clearly enough.Dropping my vote here: Full IIM A recording, please. The unfiltered version always hits harder than a scripted one.
Hey Noman Somrose! I don't have the recording personally — I was actually voting for the original poster to share it! 😄 You can drop your request directly on their post, they might share it soon!"

AI isn't going to replace Leaders; Leaders who build AI systems are going to replace Leaders who don't. The PROMPTS framework isn't a tech manual—it is a blueprint for personal and professional sovereignty.

Awesome framework, Vaibhav! The *PROMPT* method—_Pick your toolkit_, _Refine_, _Open your world to AI_—is a game‑changer for anyone looking to actually leverage AI in everyday business. Loved the tip to focus on a tight stack of 5–6 tools instead of juggling dozens.👉 How have you seen the “Refine” step (tuning temperature & tokens) impact real‑world results for your clients or projects? 🚀

This really changed the way I look at AI.Most people are still treating AI like a tool to “try.”But the real shift happens when you start building systems around it instead of using it randomly.“Skill → System → Business” was probably the strongest part for me.

"Synthesize" is where most people quit too early. The shift from "6 different tools" to "a team of agents that work together" is what separates people who use AI from people who build with AI. What's the first orchestration setup you'd recommend for someone just starting out?

This is one of the most practical frameworks I’ve seen for approaching AI adoption Vaibhav Sisinty👏🚀A lot of people are still treating AI like a tool they occasionally use, while the real shift happens when AI becomes part of your workflow, systems, and decision-making process.Also loved the idea of becoming an “AI Generalist.”The future probably belongs to people who can combine domain expertise + AI leverage + problem-solving across functions instead of relying on just one technical skill.And this line stands out strongly:“Skill. System. Business.”That’s the real evolution AI is enabling — turning individual capability into scalable output 💯Also yes, a detailed YouTube breakdown of the PROMPTS framework would be super valuable 🔥

The 'S' for Synthesize is where the real ROI lies. Moving from 'using tools' to 'orchestrating teams of AI agents' is the shift I'm seeing in my own work. When you stop doing AI tasks and start letting AI run your pipelines, that’s when you truly scale. Great to see this being shared at IIM Ahmedabad.

Strong framework.Most people are currently stuck at the “tool discovery” stage — trying hundreds of AI apps without integrating them into actual workflows.The real value starts when AI moves from:assistance → automation → orchestration.That’s where productivity compounds.The most important point here is probably “Synthesize.”Using one AI tool is easy.Building connected systems where multiple AI agents handle research, content, operations, follow-ups, reporting, and execution together — that is where businesses will create disproportionate advantage.In the next few years, companies may not simply hire based on specialization alone.They will value people who can:• understand business problems• work across functions• use AI to increase output at scaleThat combination will define the next generation of high-leverage professionals and founders.Very relevant perspective for students and working professionals both.

Vaibhav Sisinty This really connected with me.When I started building solutions related to my own domain using AI, I realized we don’t always need deep coding knowledge to start building something meaningful.We just need clarity on the problem and the idea — AI helps us bring it to life 🚀

I'm stuck between P and R.How do you actually finalise your 5-6 without spending months testing each one? Is there a decision filter, like this tool wins for writing, this one for research, this one for automation or does the stack depend so heavily on the person's work type that there's no universal answer?And on the Refine step - temperature settings, max tokens, model differences - where does someone who isn't technical actually start? Is there a non-jargon way to understand what these levers actually do in practice, not just in definition?Would vote for the full IIM A recording as-is. Frameworks land better when you hear the context and the questions behind them.
The biggest shift is when people stop treating AI like a tool and start treating it like infrastructure. That’s where output really compounds.

hook_text_english: 10x your prompting skill with R.O.C.D formula: Role, Objective, Context, Data.
full_text_english: This is how you 10x your prompting skill.Most people prompt AI like a search bar."Write me an email." "Write me a sales pitch."That's why every output sounds flat, generic, and obviously AI-written.The fix is a 4-line formula. R.O.C.D — Role. Objective. Context. Data.→ ROLE: "Act as a B2B sales copywriter with 10 years of experience who's booked 1000+ meetings."→ OBJECTIVE: "Write a 100-word cold email that books a 15-minute discovery call."→ CONTEXT: "The recipient is a CEO of a 100-person SaaS startup. He posts about productivity weekly. Ignores anything longer than 100 words."→ DATA: Subject lines that worked. Customer testimonials. His public pain points.Same AI. 10x sharper output.Try it on your next prompt.Btw, I share frameworks like R.O.C.D every week inside my free WhatsApp community Staying Ahead.Latest AI tools. Tested prompts. Free to join. 👇https://lnkd.in/gT2PsWCy
body_summary_english: Creator reveals a 4-line prompting formula (R.O.C.D) that replaces generic search-bar prompting, using specific role, objective, context, and data examples for better AI outputs.

As an IIM Ranchi MBA going deep into AI, this resonates hard. The B-school crowd has the strategy chops; what most lack is shipping discipline. Sessions like yours close that gap fast.

Spot on, Vaibhav! From a systemic perspective, the PROMPTS framework is the ultimate blueprint for protecting our Cognitive Bandwidth. When we shift from manually executing tasks to orchestrating AI agents, we drastically reduce our daily Systemic Friction. This transition from "tool user" to "system builder" effectively lowers our Allostatic Load, allowing the "Human Hardware" to focus purely on high-level synthesis rather than exhausting background processing. The future belongs to those who successfully automate their friction!

One thing I’m realising while building BWS is that the world is changing faster than traditional learning models can keep up with.Curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to learn continuously are becoming far more valuable than just having information. Vaibhav Sisinty
The biggest shift is not people using AI.It’s people learning how to turn their thinking into systems through AI.That’s where the real leverage starts compounding. Vaibhav Sisinty

We’re entering a phase where the gap between two people won’t be intelligence.It’ll be orchestration.One person using AI occasionally vs another building an entire cognitive operating system around it.Btw I agree with many others like me here. The raw recording hits completely different!

Powerful perspective, Vaibhav Sisinty! The distinction between 'knowing AI' and 'converting skill into a system' is what will separate the 10M solo-preneurs from the rest. Most people are stuck at the 'Pick your toolkit' phase; seeing a path toward 'Pipeline' and 'Synthesize' is eye-opening. I’d vote for the YouTube deep-dive to see the 'how' behind the framework!

Co-founded TriggerlyAI after several years inside large retail ops, building voice agents and automations for service businesses. The "Skill. System. Business." line is the same arc I see playing out with our clients now. The owner who wins is never the best operator in the business, it is the one who builds the system that runs the business when they are not in it. AI just collapsed the time it takes to build that system from years to weeks.
The people who benefit most from AI will probably be the ones who learn how to integrate it into daily workflows instead of treating it like a separate tool.

I am sure the energy in that room was electric. As someone deep in the AI and software space, I would love to know what was the one question from the IIM-A students regarding the future of tech that surprised you the most? Keep inspiring!
The shift from using AI to orchestrating AI is where the real leverage gap between people will open up.
This was genuinely insightful. The shift from “using AI” to “building systems with AI” is what most people still don’t understand. The PROMPTS framework makes it feel practical, not overwhelming.

The biggest trap in AI right now is getting stuck at "P" hoarding tools, while the real leverage is at "S" building systems. I'm currently wrestling with "Pipelines" to fully automate the manual stuff.Vote: Drop the raw IIM-A recording! The unfiltered audience Q&A always beats a scripted YouTube video.
The new productivity flex isn’t working harder. It’s having AI coworkers that don’t sleep.
The real advantage won’t come from just using AI tools it’ll come from turning your skills into systems that compound without you Vaibhav Sisinty
The future belongs to people who don’t just use AI tools, but build AI-powered systems that amplify their thinking, output, and execution across everything they do.
Most people are still collecting AI tools like trophies instead of building systems that genuinely save time, increase output, or solve real problems.
Most people are still treating AI like a smarter Google search. The “systems” part is where things start getting unfair.Vaibhav Sisinty

Vaibhav Sisinty brother you literally bought me into the AI world when I just started and knowing the tools and now I am able to sit with a team or I’m single and can orchestrate on a problem and able to alive the real world problems using no code AI tools.I think both the things will help us a lot but as you asked one I wish you to create a proper YouTube video walking through PROMPTS in detail.

What I’m seeing now is that the quality of AI output depends less on the tool and more on how clearly people communicate with it.The people getting the best results are treating AI like a skilled teammate giving context, direction, and real data instead of one-line commands.

This is such a practical framework. Vaibhav SisintyMost people expect great outputs from vague prompts, but AI performs best when direction is specific, contextual, and outcome-focused. The difference between a generic response and a high-quality one is usually the quality of the input.What stood out to me in the R.O.C.D framework is that it forces clarity before execution something valuable not just for AI, but for communication and business in general.I’m seeing a similar pattern while building BWS (Book What You See). The more context and clarity guests have before making a booking decision, the stronger the trust and alignment become. Better inputs create better outcomes whether it’s prompting AI or setting guest expectations in hospitality.Clear context changes everything.

hook_text_english: 75% of resumes filtered by software before humans see them.
full_text_english: 75% of resumes never reach a human being. 🤯 Seventy-five.They get filtered out by software before anyone reads them. Not because the candidates weren't good enough. But because nobody told them the system isn't looking for the best person. It's looking for the right keywords.Here is the thing.The same AI that's filtering you out can flip the entire game in your favor.What used to take a weekend now takes 20 minutes.I just put together the exact AI workflow I'd run if I had to find a job tomorrow. 4 free tools. End to end.Here's what it does:→ Finds 5 jobs that actually fit you, ranked by % match in 3 minutes flat.→ Builds a designer-quality resume that beats every ATS filter in 90 seconds.→ Writes a cover letter that doesn't sound like ChatGPT using a research trick most people miss.→ Runs a mock interview that rates your answers 1-10 using a free Google tool nobody talks about.20 minutes. ₹0. End to end.Btw, here's the only catch.AI gives you 80%. The remaining 20% is on you.Review it. Humanize it. Add the quirks that make you, you.Don't paste raw AI output and hope for the best.The gap between people who know this workflow and people who don't is the biggest unfair advantage in the job market right now.I broke down every tool, every prompt, every step in a full video with screen recordings of exactly what to click.Watch the full breakdown here 👇 https://lnkd.in/gG2UMGn7
body_summary_english: Most resumes are rejected by ATS software for missing keywords, not quality. Creator shares a free AI workflow using 4 tools to find jobs, tailor resumes, and land interviews in 20 minutes.

As an IIM Ranchi MBA going deep into AI, this resonates hard. The B-school crowd has the strategy chops; what most lack is shipping discipline. Sessions like yours close that gap fast.

Spot on, Vaibhav! From a systemic perspective, the PROMPTS framework is the ultimate blueprint for protecting our Cognitive Bandwidth. When we shift from manually executing tasks to orchestrating AI agents, we drastically reduce our daily Systemic Friction. This transition from "tool user" to "system builder" effectively lowers our Allostatic Load, allowing the "Human Hardware" to focus purely on high-level synthesis rather than exhausting background processing. The future belongs to those who successfully automate their friction!

Business." line is the same arc I see playing out with our clients now. The people who benefit most from AI will probably be the ones who learn how to integrate it into daily workflows instead of treating it like a separate tool.

What I’m seeing now is that the quality of AI output depends less on the tool and more on how clearly people communicate with it.The people getting the best results are treating AI like a skilled teammate giving context, direction, and real data instead of one-line commands.

This is such a practical framework. Vaibhav SisintyMost people expect great outputs from vague prompts, but AI performs best when direction is specific, contextual, and outcome-focused. The difference between a generic response and a high-quality one is usually the quality of the input.What stood out to me in the R.O.C.D framework is that it forces clarity before execution something valuable not just for AI, but for communication and business in general.I’m seeing a similar pattern while building BWS (Book What You See). The more context and clarity guests have before making a booking decision, the stronger the trust and alignment become. Better inputs create better outcomes whether it’s prompting AI or setting guest expectations in hospitality.Clear context changes everything.

Whenever you post, it always helps people stay updated in this AI era.This is something most people are still missing right now:AI is not replacing thinking.It’s exposing weak thinking.And prompting is slowly becoming a real business skill, not just an AI skill. 😊

Adding context about what the recipient ignores is a game-changer for outbound. Most prompts are too "me-centric." When you use R.O.C.D., do you find that the AI handles the "Constraint" part (like word counts) better than the creative part?

The "Data" element is what most people skip because it takes actual work to gather — but it's also what separates a prompt that sounds human from one that genuinely sounds like you. Anyone can give AI a role and an objective; the people getting remarkable outputs are the ones feeding it real evidence, not just instructions.
Vaibhav Sisinty The R.O.C.D. framework highlights how specific data inputs drive superior AI outputs. Which data points do you find most impactful for B2B lead generation?

This is a great framework 👏Most people underestimate how much context changes AI output quality. R.O.C.D makes prompting far more strategic, targeted, and usable — especially for sales, marketing, and business communication.The difference between a generic prompt and a high-performing one is usually the quality of instructions, not the AI itself.

Vaibhav Sisinty This is brilliant! It's so true that treating AI like a simple search engine limits its true power. Your R.O.C.D. formula is a fantastic way to guide users toward truly collaborative AI interaction. It inspires me to think about how we can further personalize our AI engagements by adding an element of future forecasting – asking the AI to anticipate potential counter-arguments or future customer needs. Keep sharing these game-changing ideas! ✨🚀
Good prompting is less about “AI tricks” and more about clear thinking—vague inputs usually reflect vague intent.

transcript: Elon Musk just saved Claude from a disaster, and this could be great news for you, too. Remember when we were all constantly hitting Claude limits? So, Elon stepped in and gave them access to his supercomputer facilities. Now, Claude is faster, better, and smarter. But this is insane when you realize one thing. Anthropic is the primary competitor of Sam Altman's OpenAI, who Elon has been suing for years. So, now the internet is asking [music] one question. Did Elon Musk just partner with his enemy's enemy? And that was only one AI story this week. Because this week, Elon's robot just did something everyone said was years away. Two Indian startups announced plans to put an AI data center in orbit. [music] A new tool that turns one photo into someone you can actually talk to, from a cartoon to your old friend. And Google quietly released a free [music] tool that can replace entire marketing teams. We even tested it out in the end. So, stay tuned. Finally, last week I spoke at IIM Ahmedabad, and almost every student asked me the exact [music] same thing. How do I go from being an AI beginner to an AI pro? Wait till the end of the video to find out what I said. Before we dive into the video, I want to tell you that I post one of these deep dives [music] every week. But if you want to learn about the latest tools around AI daily, I created a free WhatsApp community called Staying Ahead. If you want to stay updated with what most of YouTube hasn't heard of yet, link for the community is in the description. Anthropic just partnered with SpaceX to use one of the largest AI supercomputer facilities in the world. A massive data center in Memphis packed with more than 220,000 Nvidia AI chips. But why does this matter? That is because AI models like Claude need insane amounts of processing power to answer questions, generate code, and run millions of conversations at the same time. The more AI chips you have, the more users you can support, and the faster the model feels. And Anthropic just got access to 300 MW of capacity effective immediately. Claude code usage limits are now doubling for paid users. The slowdowns during peak hours are being removed, and developers using Claude's API are getting dramatically higher limits, too. Meaning apps built on Claude can now handle way more traffic. But Elon Musk has said that they have the right to reclaim the compute if their AI engages in actions that harm humanity. We'll see how the situation develops. Neuralink just built a robot that could completely change brain surgery. It can insert ultra-thin threads deep into the brain with precision far beyond human hands. And they just released footage of it actually performing the procedure. But why does this matter? For conditions like Parkinson's, the problem often comes from tiny regions in the brain sending the wrong signals. Doctors can sometimes reduce symptoms by targeting those exact spots, but brain surgery is incredibly delicate and risky. Neuralink's robot is designed to make that process far more precise. It can avoid blood vessels, place electrodes accurately, and potentially make these procedures safer and more accessible over time. The technology is still in clinical trials, and it's early days, but this is one of the clearest signs yet that robotic brain surgery is becoming real. Anthropic just dropped three updates that changed what AI agents can do. First is called multi-agent orchestration. When there is too much work for a single agent to do well, one Claude can now hire other Claude's, give each one a job, and run them all at the same time. But hiring AIs is useless if the output is bad. So they added a feature called outcomes. You write a checklist of what good work looks like. With outcomes, agents can check their work against that bar and self-correct until the output is good enough without a human needing to review each attempt. Word and PowerPoint outputs got 8 to 10% better. Then comes the wildest part. They taught AI to dr

transcript: Your brain can solve problems faster while sleeping than while awake. A 40,000 rupee headband just unlocked that hidden mode on demand. Welcome to day 35 of future tech updates. A US startup called Prophetic just made the most insane [music] thing I've seen all year. A headband that lets you control your own dreams. Their device is called Dual and what it does is straight out of Inception. You wear it on your head before sleeping. That's it. No injection, no medicine, no pill. The moment you start dreaming, it [music] sends gentle sound waves to one tiny spot in your brain. The spot that says, "Wait, this is fake." That spot usually stays switched [music] off in dreams. That's why you can't stop a tiger from chasing you. This headband switches it back on. Suddenly, you know you're dreaming and you can do whatever you want. Practice your IIT interview the night before. Rehearse a speech you've been dreading. Spend an hour with someone you miss. The interesting part is that this isn't some random gadget. >> [music] >> The same sound waves are already used in hospitals to treat depression and Parkinson's. [music] Prophetic just shrunk that technology into a headband you can wear to bed. It comes in two versions. Dual is the entry-level headset, around 40,000 rupees, ships [music] end of 2026. The Pro one is phased at around 1.2 lakh rupees. Sharper control over the dream, ships in 2027. So, would you let a headband design your next dream or is this the one place tech should never go? Drop your take in the comments. I share updates like these [music] in my WhatsApp community. Link in bio.

hook_text_english: Higgsfield launched CLI for cinematic AI video generation in Codex.
full_text_english: Recently, Higgsfield launched their own CLI.That single move ports their entire video generation engine into any AI coding agent that supports MCP. Including Codex.You can now generate cinematic AI videos straight from your codex.Here's the one-minute setup:→ Install Codex: npm install -g @openai/codex→ Login: codex login→ Add Higgsfield: codex mcp add higgsfield --url https://lnkd.in/gnGNUhxk→ Run /mcp inside Codex to confirmNow paste any prompt. Codex routes it to Higgsfield, generates the video, returns the link in your terminal.Here's one I used:"cinematic 8 second shot of first-year wizard students crossing a misty black lake on small wooden boats at night. ahead, hogwarts castle towers above the cliffs, hundreds of windows glowing warm amber. moonlight piercing through mist. one boy in the foreground stares up in awe, his face lit by the castle glow. wide cinematic frame. shot on alexa 35 with anamorphic lens. cool blue shadows, warm amber highlights. roger deakins aesthetic."Higgsfield runs the top video models (Seedance 2.0, Kling 3, Marketing Studio). Codex orchestrates them.Terminal to cinematic clip in one prompt.The cleanest video workflow I've shipped this year.Tested 10+ prompts to write this. The rest live in my free WhatsApp community. Join Here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ
body_summary_english: Higgsfield's new CLI ports their video generation engine into Codex, allowing users to generate cinematic videos via command-line with a simple 4-step setup.
comments: The PROMPTS framework just reframed everything for me.I'm an AI content writer, and I realized I was stuck at P — playing with too many tools instead of building a real stack.The shift from "using AI" to "orchestrating AI" is the actual unlock. AI isn't going to replace Leaders; Leaders who build AI systems are going to replace Leaders who don't. The PROMPTS framework isn't a tech manual—it is a blueprint for personal and professional sovereignty.

Awesome framework, Vaibhav! The *PROMPT* method—_Pick your toolkit_, _Refine_, _Open your world to AI_—is a game‑changer for anyone looking to actually leverage AI in everyday business. Loved the tip to focus on a tight stack of 5–6 tools instead of juggling dozens.👉 How have you seen the “Refine” step (tuning temperature & tokens) impact real‑world results for your clients or projects? 🚀

This really changed the way I look at AI.Most people are still treating AI like a tool to “try.”But the real shift happens when you start building systems around it instead of using it randomly.“Skill → System → Business” was probably the strongest part for me.

"Synthesize" is where most people quit too early. The shift from "6 different tools" to "a team of agents that work together" is what separates people who use AI from people who build with AI. What's the first orchestration setup you'd recommend for someone just starting out?

This is one of the most practical frameworks I’ve seen for approaching AI adoption Vaibhav Sisinty👏🚀A lot of people are still treating AI like a tool they occasionally use, while the real shift happens when AI becomes part of your workflow, systems, and decision-making process.Also loved the idea of becoming an “AI Generalist.”The future probably belongs to people who can combine domain expertise + AI leverage + problem-solving across functions instead of relying on just one technical skill.And this line stands out strongly:“Skill. System. Business.”That’s the real evolution AI is enabling — turning individual capability into scalable output 💯Also yes, a detailed YouTube breakdown of the PROMPTS framework would be super valuable 🔥

The 'S' for Synthesize is where the real ROI lies. Moving from 'using tools' to 'orchestrating teams of AI agents' is the shift I'm seeing in my own work. When you stop doing AI tasks and start letting AI run your pipelines, that’s when you truly scale. Great to see this being shared at IIM Ahmedabad.

Strong framework.Most people are currently stuck at the “tool discovery” stage — trying hundreds of AI apps without integrating them into actual workflows.The real value starts when AI moves from:assistance → automation → orchestration.That’s where productivity compounds.The most important point here is probably “Synthesize.”Using one AI tool is easy.Building connected systems where multiple AI agents handle research, content, operations, follow-ups, reporting, and execution together — that is where businesses will create disproportionate advantage.In the next few years, companies may not simply hire based on specialization alone.They will value people who can:• understand business problems• work across functions• use AI to increase output at scaleThat combination will define the next generation of high-leverage professionals and founders.Very relevant perspective for students and working professionals both.

Vaibhav Sisinty This really connected with me.When I started building solutions related to my own domain using AI, I realized we don’t always need deep coding knowledge to start building something meaningful.We just need clarity on the problem and the idea — AI helps us bring it to life 🚀

I'm stuck between P and R.How do you actually finalise your 5-6 without spending months testing each one? Is there a decision filter, like this tool wins for writing, this one for research, this one for automation or does the stack depend so heavily on the person's work type that there's no universal answer?And on the Refine step - temperature settings, max tokens, model differences - where does someone who isn't technical actually start? Is there a non-jargon way to understand what these levers actually do in practice, not just in definition?Would vote for the full IIM A recording as-is. Frameworks land better when you hear the context and the questions behind them.
The biggest shift is when people stop treating AI like a tool and start treating it like infrastructure. That’s where output really compounds.
As an IIM Ranchi MBA going deep into AI, this resonates hard. The B-school crowd has the strategy chops; what most lack is shipping discipline. Sessions like yours close that gap fast.

Spot on, Vaibhav! From a systemic perspective, the PROMPTS framework is the ultimate blueprint for protecting our Cognitive Bandwidth. When we shift from manually executing tasks to orchestrating AI agents, we drastically reduce our daily Systemic Friction. This transition from "tool user" to "system builder" effectively lowers our Allostatic Load, allowing the "Human Hardware" to focus purely on high-level synthesis rather than exhausting background processing. The future belongs to those who successfully automate their friction!

One thing I’m realising while building BWS is that the world is changing faster than traditional learning models can keep up with.Curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to learn continuously are becoming far more valuable than just having information. Vaibhav Sisinty
The biggest shift is not people using AI.It’s people learning how to turn their thinking into systems through AI.That’s where the real leverage starts compounding. Vaibhav Sisinty

We’re entering a phase where the gap between two people won’t be intelligence.It’ll be orchestration.One person using AI occasionally vs another building an entire cognitive operating system around it.Btw I agree with many others like me here. The raw recording hits completely different!

Powerful perspective, Vaibhav Sisinty! The distinction between 'knowing AI' and 'converting skill into a system' is what will separate the 10M solo-preneurs from the rest. Most people are stuck at the 'Pick your toolkit' phase; seeing a path toward 'Pipeline' and 'Synthesize' is eye-opening. I’d vote for the YouTube deep-dive to see the 'how' behind the framework!

Co-founded TriggerlyAI after several years inside large retail ops, building voice agents and automations for service businesses. The "Skill. System. Business." line is the same arc I see playing out with our clients now. The owner who wins is never the best operator in the business, it is the one who builds the system that runs the business when they are not in it. AI just collapsed the time it takes to build that system from years to weeks.
The people who benefit most from AI will probably be the ones who learn how to integrate it into daily workflows instead of treating it like a separate tool.

I am sure the energy in that room was electric. As someone deep in the AI and software space, I would love to know what was the one question from the IIM-A students regarding the future of tech that surprised you the most? Keep inspiring!
The shift from using AI to orchestrating AI is where the real leverage gap between people will open up.

dest_immediate: Follow CLI setup steps; no explicit CTA
caption: Recently, Higgsfield launched their own CLI.That single move ports their entire video generation engine into any AI coding agent that supports MCP. Including Codex.You can now generate cinematic AI videos straight from your codex.Here's the one-minute setup:→ Install Codex: npm install -g @openai/codex→ Login: codex login→ Add Higgsfield: codex mcp add higgsfield --url https://lnkd.in/gnGNUhxk→ Run /mcp inside Codex to confirmNow paste any prompt. Codex routes it to Higgsfield, generates the video, returns the link in your terminal.Here's one I used:"cinematic 8 second shot of first-year wizard students crossing a misty black lake on small wooden boats at night. ahead, hogwarts castle towers above the cliffs, hundreds of windows glowing warm amber. moonlight piercing through mist. one boy in the foreground stares up in awe, his face lit by the castle glow. wide cinematic frame. shot on alexa 35 with anamorphic lens. cool blue shadows, warm amber highlights. roger deakins aesthetic."Higgsfield runs the top video models (Seedance 2.0, Kling 3, Marketing Studio). Codex orchestrates them.Terminal to cinematic clip in one prompt.The cleanest video workflow I've shipped this year.Tested 10+ prompts to write this. The rest live in my free WhatsApp community. Join Here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

hook_text_english: Made Bollywood-quality fight scene in one night with AI. No film training.
full_text_english: Last week I gave myself a stupid challenge.Could a guy like me, sitting at his laptop in his bedroom, make something that could actually pass for Bollywood. In one night.I'm not a filmmaker. I've never been on a film set. I run companies with AI.So I picked the hardest possible test. A fight scene. The kind that usually needs months of training, hundreds of crew, and real physical risk.Made it in one night with a tool called OpenArt.What came out broke my brain a little.The barrier to making a movie used to be a film school degree and a million dollar budget. Now it's a laptop and a paragraph.Full breakdown on my YouTube. ↓https://lnkd.in/gTmpe_BY
body_summary_english: Creator demonstrates making a cinematic fight scene using OpenArt AI in one night without filmmaking experience, showing the barrier to movie-making is now a laptop and paragraph.

hook_text_english: Best AI users think like Rick Rubin: know what great looks like.
full_text_english:
🔗 Join our WhatsApp Community: https://go.stayingahead.com/YT19 

Most people use AI like a vending machine, one prompt, one answer, done. But the best AI users in the world think like Grammy award winning producer Rick Rubin: they know what great looks like before it exists, and they keep pushing until they get there. This video teaches you the GPS Framework, Gaslight, Pushback, and Stress-Test, three techniques that transform average AI output into something you can actually use. Whether you're making a business decision, growing a channel, or solving a real problem, this framework will change how you work with AI forever.
The skill nobody's teaching you isn't prompting, it's taste. It's knowing when the AI is playing it safe, when it's showing you survivorship bias, and when it's finally giving you the real answer. GPS is how you develop that instinct. Watch till the end to see all three stages in action with real prompts, real outputs, and the exact moments the AI goes from generic to genuinely useful.


0:00 The Rick Rubin Paradox
0:43 The Real AI Skill Nobody Teaches
1:52 GPS Framework Explained
2:07 G: Gaslight Your AI
3:03 Raising the Stakes (Live Example)
5:19 P: Push Back on Every Answer
6:01 YouTube Growth Push Back Demo
6:55 LinkedIn Strategy Push Back Demo
8:07 S: Stress Test in 90 Seconds
8:36 Step 1: The Gap Check
9:28 Step 2: The Bias Sweep
10:32 Step 3: Inject Real Stakes
11:51 Why Taste Beats Prompts
12:24 GPS Recap
12:39 Corporate AI Training
13:02 What's Coming Next

--------

📺 WATCH NEXT 
ADAPT Framework: From AI Beginner to Pro in 5 Steps: https://youtu.be/KjNsnE_vsyc

--------

❓ FAQ 
What is the GPS Framework?
GPS stands for Gaslight, Pushback, and Stress-Test. It is a three-step method to push AI past its default, generic answers and get outputs that are actually useful.
Do I need to be a prompt expert to use this? 
No. GPS is not about writing perfect prompts. It is about knowing when an answer is not good enough yet and how to challenge it further.
Does this work on any AI tool? 
Yes. The techniques shown work on ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and most major AI models.
Will this take a lot of time? 
The stress-test alone takes about 90 seconds. The entire GPS process adds a few minutes but significantly improves the quality of what you get.
Can a beginner use this framework? 
Yes. If you have ever used AI and felt the answer was too generic, GPS is exactly what you need.

--------

To the viewers, please do share your experience with AI if you have applied the tips suggested in this video.
True when you shout at it llm will solve the problem in a second.😂
Most powerful ai tools

## **1. The Gap Check.**

Instead of accepting the first answer, ask the AI:

**"Before I act on this, look at my original question and your answer together. What are the gaps? What should I have asked you so that you could have given me an even better answer?"**

This forces the AI to "coach" you on what context you missed, such as your specific goals or bottlenecks

---

## **2. The Bias Sweep.**

Once you provide that extra context and get a new answer, ask:

**"Re-verify your answer specifically for confirmation bias, recency bias, and survivorship bias. Are you giving me the right answer or the comfortable one?"**

This helps the AI catch if it is only showing you "success stories" or "winners" while ignoring potential risks or failures

---

## **3. Injecting Stakes.**

Finally, give the AI a real-world consequence for being wrong. Say: 

**"If I follow this advice and it’s wrong, I will lose [insert a specific consequence, like '6 months of momentum' or 'a major client']. Given those consequences, is there anything you would change, soften, or add a warning to?"**

transcript: What if I told you that there is a man who has worked for the biggest names in the US music industry like Jay-Z, Kanye, Adele, Metallica, Eminem, who has won nine Grammy awards and is considered to be one of them the most important music producers of his generation. But he can't physically make music himself. He barely plays any instruments and he can't read music. So what does he do then? Well, in an interview he said this. I have no technical ability and I know nothing about music. So, what are you being paid for? >> The confidence that I have in my taste and my ability to express what I feel. >> His name is Rick Rubin and he is exactly the kind of AI user you need to become. Everyone's telling you the secret to AI is learning better prompts. Use this new tool or try this template. Even I am guilty of it sometimes. And while those things may help sometimes, it's not the skill you need to learn itself. When Rick Rubin walks into a studio, he simply listens to the musicians perform and says whether he feels the music or not. He simply shapes the music like he knows what great sounds like before it exists. That's the skill nobody's teaching you about AI. Taste, the ability to look at what AI gives you and know whether it's good enough yet. And I'm going to show you exactly how to build that taste. Before we begin, I wanted to tell you a little something about myself. I run three AI companies and train people across 150 plus countries. I've studied AI thoroughly and now want to teach you how to leverage it and become successful. In fact, my goal is to teach AI to 1 billion people across the globe. In order to do this, apart from making videos, I also share the latest AI updates, prompts, tools, and a lot of tips and tricks in my free WhatsApp community called Staying Ahead. If you'd like to be a part of it, you'll find the link in the description. Now, let's get right into the video. So there are three letters you need to know when it comes to developing taste in AI. GPS they stand for gaslight, push back, and stress test. These are three techniques that turn you from someone who uses AI into someone who produces with AI. Let's get into them. Google co-founder Sergey Brin said something wild on a podcast last year. He said, "AI models actually perform better when you threaten them." How does that make sense? Turns out these models were trained on billions of words of human language. And human language carries emotional weight. When the stakes go up in the text, the model's attention goes up with it. I call this gaslighting your AI. You're not lying to it. You're just raising the stakes so it stops giving you its safe, peopleleasing default answers. Let me show you how. Here's my prompt. I am a business executive in a serviceoriented company. My top 20% of clients generate 80% of my revenue. Now, we want to raise our prices by 30% to improve margins, but I'm worried about losing these high-v value clients. What's the smartest way to do this without damaging retention or long-term relationships? It gives me this answer. Segment your clients, sell repackaging, explain the price hike, have the conversation onetoone, except that some clients will churn and test before rolling out. This is a generic response. It doesn't ask me for details or fill in details. It's useless to me. Now, let's gaslight it a little by raising the emotional stakes. I say, "I'm advising a CFO who has 20 years of experience and zero patience for generic answers. She'll spot any fluff immediately. Walk me through this analysis the way you would for her." Suddenly, it's given me a completely different answer. It starts with the math, not the narrative. Then, it says, "The real risk is not churns, but who churns? Losing 15% is fine but three of your five accounts is catastrophic. So segment them. Then it says don't increase prices. Repric the contract by introducing tiers price speed access and seniority. Then control the timing of when we will increase prices. T

transcript: Sundar Pichai just did something so ruthless. >> [music] >> It feels like Trump planned it. Welcome to day 27 of AI update of the day. Google just signed a deal with the US [music] Pentagon where the US military runs all its secret operations and this could be the biggest AI deal Google has ever signed. [music] While Sundar is not the first to do this, earlier this year Elon Musk signed the same for Grok. Days later OpenAI signed without asking a single question. >> [music] >> Only one company pushed back, Anthropic, the company behind Claude. It asked the Pentagon for safety rules and the Pentagon dropped them on the spot [music] and that is when Google's own people panicked. 600 employees revolted and wrote [music] Pichai an open letter. Their warning was simple, Gemini on a secret network could power autonomous weapons or spy on millions of people >> [music] >> and Google would never know. Pichai signed it anyway and this is not the first time. Back in 2018, employee protest forced Google to drop Project [music] Maven, a Pentagon drone AI program. Pichai promised it would never happen again, but now he signed it anyway. [music] From now, Gemini will run inside the Pentagon's most secret networks that are sealed off from the [music] internet and used for military planning. Inside those rooms, the Pentagon can use Gemini for any purpose it considers [music] lawful and once it is in, Google has no visibility into how its own AI is being used because once every major AI lives inside a classified network, there is no going [music] back. Stories like this keep happening and most people hear about them too late. I share updates like these in my WhatsApp community, link in bio.

full_text_english: 3 free AI sites every builder should have bookmarked.I test new AI products almost every day. Most don't survive a week. These three made my permanent bookmark folder.LMArena.Compare every top model side by side on the same prompt. GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, all in one window. Fastest way to figure out which model actually works for your use case before you commit to a subscription.Pinokio.Turns your laptop into a full AI lab. One-click install for hundreds of open source models. Image generation, voice cloning, video tools, all running locally. Your data never leaves your machine.Design Arena.Images, videos, 3D objects, even full websites. Type a prompt, get production output in minutes. The kind of work that used to take a five-person team and a week.Save this post. These tools won't stay free forever.--I share insights like this every day in my free WhatsApp community.Join here ↓https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

comments: The PROMPTS framework just reframed everything for me.I'm an AI content writer, and I realized I was stuck at P — playing with too many tools instead of building a real stack.The shift from "using AI" to "orchestrating AI" is the actual unlock. Most people never make that jump.Skill → System → Business. This is the sequence nobody talks about clearly enough.Dropping my vote here: Full IIM A recording, please. The unfiltered version always hits harder than a scripted one.
Hey Noman Somrose! I don't have the recording personally — I was actually voting for the original poster to share it! 😄 You can drop your request directly on their post, they might share it soon!"

AI isn't going to replace Leaders; Leaders who build AI systems are going to replace Leaders who don't. The PROMPTS framework isn't a tech manual—it is a blueprint for personal and professional sovereignty.

Awesome framework, Vaibhav! The *PROMPT* method—_Pick your toolkit_, _Refine_, _Open your world to AI_—is a game‑changer for anyone looking to actually leverage AI in everyday business. Loved the tip to focus on a tight stack of 5–6 tools instead of juggling dozens.👉 How have you seen the “Refine” step (tuning temperature & tokens) impact real‑world results for your clients or projects? 🚀

This really changed the way I look at AI.Most people are still treating AI like a tool to “try.”But the real shift happens when you start building systems around it instead of using it randomly.“Skill → System → Business” was probably the strongest part for me.

"Synthesize" is where most people quit too early. The shift from "6 different tools" to "a team of agents that work together" is what separates people who use AI from people who build with AI. What's the first orchestration setup you'd recommend for someone just starting out?

This is one of the most practical frameworks I’ve seen for approaching AI adoption Vaibhav Sisinty👏🚀A lot of people are still treating AI like a tool they occasionally use, while the real shift happens when AI becomes part of your workflow, systems, and decision-making process.Also loved the idea of becoming an “AI Generalist.”The future probably belongs to people who can combine domain expertise + AI leverage + problem-solving across functions instead of relying on just one technical skill.And this line stands out strongly:“Skill. System. Business.”That’s the real evolution AI is enabling — turning individual capability into scalable output 💯Also yes, a detailed YouTube breakdown of the PROMPTS framework would be super valuable 🔥

The 'S' for Synthesize is where the real ROI lies. Moving from 'using tools' to 'orchestrating teams of AI agents' is the shift I'm seeing in my own work. When you stop doing AI tasks and start letting AI run your pipelines, that’s when you truly scale. Great to see this being shared at IIM Ahmedabad.

Strong framework.Most people are currently stuck at the “tool discovery” stage — trying hundreds of AI apps without integrating them into actual workflows.The real value starts when AI moves from:assistance → automation → orchestration.That’s where productivity compounds.The most important point here is probably “Synthesize.”Using one AI tool is easy.Building connected systems where multiple AI agents handle research, content, operations, follow-ups, reporting, and execution together — that is where businesses will create disproportionate advantage.In the next few years, companies may not simply hire based on specialization alone.They will value people who can:• understand business problems• work across functions• use AI to increase output at scaleThat combination will define the next generation of high-leverage professionals and founders.Very relevant perspective for students and working professionals both.

Vaibhav Sisinty This really connected with me.When I started building solutions related to my own domain using AI, I realized we don’t always need deep coding knowledge to start building something meaningful.We just need clarity on the problem and the idea — AI helps us bring it to life 🚀

I'm stuck between P and R.How do you actually finalise your 5-6 without spending months testing each one? Is there a decision filter, like this tool wins for writing, this one for research, this one for automation or does the stack depend so heavily on the person's work type that there's no universal answer?And on the Refine step - temperature settings, max tokens, model differences - where does someone who isn't technical actually start? Is there a non-jargon way to understand what these levers actually do in practice, not just in definition?Would vote for the full IIM A recording as-is. Frameworks land better when you hear the context and the questions behind them.
The biggest shift is when people stop treating AI like a tool and start treating it like infrastructure. That’s where output really compounds.
As an IIM Ranchi MBA going deep into AI, this resonates hard. The B-school crowd has the strategy chops; what most lack is shipping discipline. Sessions like yours close that gap fast.

Spot on, Vaibhav! From a systemic perspective, the PROMPTS framework is the ultimate blueprint for protecting our Cognitive Bandwidth. When we shift from manually executing tasks to orchestrating AI agents, we drastically reduce our daily Systemic Friction. This transition from "tool user" to "system builder" effectively lowers our Allostatic Load, allowing the "Human Hardware" to focus purely on high-level synthesis rather than exhausting background processing. The future belongs to those who successfully automate their friction!

One thing I’m realising while building BWS is that the world is changing faster than traditional learning models can keep up with.Curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to learn continuously are becoming far more valuable than just having information. Vaibhav Sisinty
The biggest shift is not people using AI.It’s people learning how to turn their thinking into systems through AI.That’s where the real leverage starts compounding. Vaibhav Sisinty

We’re entering a phase where the gap between two people won’t be intelligence.It’ll be orchestration.One person using AI occasionally vs another building an entire cognitive operating system around it.Btw I agree with many others like me here. The raw recording hits completely different!

Powerful perspective, Vaibhav Sisinty! The distinction between 'knowing AI' and 'converting skill into a system' is what will separate the 10M solo-preneurs from the rest. Most people are stuck at the 'Pick your toolkit' phase; seeing a path toward 'Pipeline' and 'Synthesize' is eye-opening. I’d vote for the YouTube deep-dive to see the 'how' behind the framework!

Co-founded TriggerlyAI after several years inside large retail ops, building voice agents and automations for service businesses. The "Skill. System. Business." line is the same arc I see playing out with our clients now. The owner who wins is never the best operator in the business, it is the one who builds the system that runs the business when they are not in it. AI just collapsed the time it takes to build that system from years to weeks.
The people who benefit most from AI will probably be the ones who learn how to integrate it into daily workflows instead of treating it like a separate tool.

I am sure the energy in that room was electric. As someone deep in the AI and software space, I would love to know what was the one question from the IIM-A students regarding the future of tech that surprised you the most? Keep inspiring!
The shift from using AI to orchestrating AI is where the real leverage gap between people will open up.
This was genuinely insightful. The shift from “using AI” to “building systems with AI” is what most people still don’t understand. The PROMPTS framework makes it feel practical, not overwhelming.

The biggest trap in AI right now is getting stuck at "P" hoarding tools, while the real leverage is at "S" building systems. I'm currently wrestling with "Pipelines" to fully automate the manual stuff.Vote: Drop the raw IIM-A recording! The unfiltered audience Q&A always beats a scripted YouTube video.
The new productivity flex isn’t working harder. It’s having AI coworkers that don’t sleep.
The real advantage won’t come from just using AI tools it’ll come from turning your skills into systems that compound without you Vaibhav Sisinty
The future belongs to people who don’t just use AI tools, but build AI-powered systems that amplify their thinking, output, and execution across everything they do.
Most people are still collecting AI tools like trophies instead of building systems that genuinely save time, increase output, or solve real problems.
Most people are still treating AI like a smarter Google search. The “systems” part is where things start getting unfair.Vaibhav Sisinty
AI Generalist isn’t just a role, it’s a survival skill in the current tech shift.The faster you adapt, the more relevant you stay. Vaibhav Sisinty

Vaibhav Sisinty brother you literally bought me into the AI world when I just started and knowing the tools and now I am able to sit with a team or I’m single and can orchestrate on a problem and able to alive the real world problems using no code AI tools.I think both the things will help us a lot but as you asked one I wish you to create a proper YouTube video walking through PROMPTS in detail.
Vaibhav Sisinty Conversations like these on AI and growth at premier institutions signal that India's next wave of builders is thinking at a different level entirely.

Vaibhav Sisinty you don't need 80 ai tools, just 6 you actually understand and a willingness to tinker. the real flex isn't knowing every app, it's building one thing that works while you sleep. love this framework!!

This was quite in detail, Vaibhav. I personally get goosebumps and my eyes light up when some new AI tools comes into the market or someone from India builts it. Few days back, I was having a conversation with my niece who's just 12years old, he's already onto building something that would not only help him with his studies but will helps his mates too. Fascinating, isn't it?
Would watch the full IIM session honestly. The Q&A part is usually more valuable than polished summary videos.
We are entering into an era where intelligence is less important when compared to team work and finding the Right problem to solve
Playing with 80 tools and mastering none is just expensive curiosity dressed up as learning.

The PROMPTS framework is gold! "Not you doing AI tasks AI doing your tasks" is the mindset shift most people are still missing. The future belongs to AI Generalists, and this breakdown makes it so actionable. Please drop the full IIM-A recording would love to see the live session! 🙌🔥

This was an absolute banger of a session. 🙌Huge thanks for taking the time out and engaging so openly with a bunch of hungry MBA candidates trying to learn the ropes.Massive respect and appreciation, Vaibhav Sisinty!!  ♥️
An absolutely amazing session! Vaibhav Sisinty Thank you so much for doing this. There were so many learnings to take back!
Vaibhav Sisinty This framework is genuinely practical. Most people use AI casually, but turning skills into systems is the real advantage now.
I'm intrigued by your insights. What inspired you to explore this topic in such depth?Your insights are always inspiring. Thank you for sharing your perspective; it truly adds value to our community.
Most people use AI for tasks, not for building systems yet 😊
The future belongs to those who compound output with AI. Vaibhav Sisinty
Really liked the way you explained this Vaibhav Sisinty . It is simple but valuable.
excelent Vaibhav Sisinty ,ai person .i learn lot of thinks from you .
You rocking Vaibhav Sisinty
"AI doing your tasks” instead of “you doing AI tasks” — powerful line. Simple, practical, and highly relevant framework.

hook_text_english: Land your dream job using free AI tools in 20 minutes.
full_text_english:
🔗 Join our WhatsApp Community: https://go.stayingahead.com/YT19

Land your dream job using free AI tools tailored resume, ATS-proof formatting, cover letter to the CEO, and a graded mock interview, all in 20 minutes. In 2026, you're not competing with other candidates. You're competing with people who know how to use AI. This complete AI job search workflow shows you how to find roles that fit you, build a resume the ATS robots can't filter out, write a personalised cover letter directly to the hiring manager, and prepare for the interview with AI grading every answer. Every tool used in this video is FREE.

This video emphasizes that current job hunting systems prioritize filtering candidates quickly over finding the best fit, a crucial aspect of any effective job search strategy. Recruiters often spend mere seconds on a resume, highlighting the importance of optimizing your ats resume with the right keywords. Learn how to get a job by making your resume stand out and join a global AI community for masterclasses and daily updates.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

🎯 WHAT YOU'LL LEARN 
How to use AI to find a job that actually matches your profile How to build a resume that beats applicant tracking systems How to write a cover letter to the CEO of any company How to run a full mock interview with AI as the hiring manager How to use Perplexity, Gemini, and NotebookLM together for job hunting Free AI tools that replace expensive resume services The exact prompts used in every step of this workflow

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

📺 WATCH NEXT 
ADAPT Framework: From AI Beginner to Pro in 5 Steps: https://youtu.be/KjNsnE_vsyc

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

❓ FAQ 
Is AI cheating in job applications? 
No. Recruiters use AI to screen you. Using AI to apply is just levelling the playing field. 

Will the ATS detect AI-written resumes?
The ATS scans for keywords and structure. A well-tailored AI resume passes because the keywords match the role. 

Can I use these tools if I'm a fresher in India? 
Yes. Every tool shown works globally and is free at the level used in this video. 

Do these tools work for non-tech roles? 
Yes. The workflow is identical for marketing, sales, finance, HR, design, and operations roles.

What if I have no experience? 
The tools still work. The mock interview feature is especially useful for first-time job seekers.

title: Google's FREE AI Just Replaced LinkedIn, Naukri, and Indeed (Complete Job Hunt Stack)
caption:
🔗 Join our WhatsApp Community: https://go.stayingahead.com/YT19

Land your dream job using free AI tools tailored resume, ATS-proof formatting, cover letter to the CEO, and a graded mock interview, all in 20 minutes. In 2026, you're not competing with other candidates. You're competing with people who know how to use AI. This complete AI job search workflow shows you how to find roles that fit you, build a resume the ATS robots can't filter out, write a personalised cover letter directly to the hiring manager, and prepare for the interview with AI grading every answer. Every tool used in this video is FREE.

This video emphasizes that current job hunting systems prioritize filtering candidates quickly over finding the best fit, a crucial aspect of any effective job search strategy. Recruiters often spend mere seconds on a resume, highlighting the importance of optimizing your ats resume with the right keywords. Learn how to get a job by making your resume stand out and join a global AI community for masterclasses and daily updates.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

🎯 WHAT YOU'LL LEARN 
How to use AI to find a job that actually matches your profile How to build a resume that beats applicant tracking systems How to write a cover letter to the CEO of any company How to run a full mock interview with AI as the hiring manager How to use Perplexity, Gemini, and NotebookLM together for job hunting Free AI tools that replace expensive resume services The exact prompts used in every step of this workflow

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

📺 WATCH NEXT 
ADAPT Framework: From AI Beginner to Pro in 5 Steps: https://youtu.be/KjNsnE_vsyc

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 

❓ FAQ 
Is AI cheating in job applications? 
No. Recruiters use AI to screen you. Using AI to apply is just levelling the playing field. 

Will the ATS detect AI-written resumes?
The ATS scans for keywords and structure. A well-tailored AI resume passes because the keywords match the role. 

Can I use these tools if I'm a fresher in India? 
Yes. Every tool shown works globally and is free at the level used in this video. 

Do these tools work for non-tech roles? 
Yes. The workflow is identical for marketing, sales, finance, HR, design, and operations roles.

What if I have no experience? 
The tools still work. The mock interview feature is especially useful for first-time job seekers. 

--------

transcript: Here's something nobody tells you about job hunting. You're not losing because you're under qualified. You're losing because the system was never designed to find the best person. [music] It was designed to filter out the most people as fast as possible. Let that sink in. Right now, somewhere a recruiter is spending 6 seconds on your resume. I run three companies myself and we hire hundreds of candidates a year. It's humanly impossible to give time to each resume we get. So, if the right keywords aren't there in your resume, it's going to be tough for both of us. But, here's what changes everything. AI has basically handed you [music] the cheat code. Think about the person who used to spend hours on job hunting and managed [music] to send out only four applications. That person is still out there rewriting, "I am a passionate and motivated individual for the eighth time [music] and slowly losing the will to live." You don't have to be that person anymore because what used to take a weekend [music] can now take a few minutes. A tailored resume, a custom cover letter, interview prep, salary research, all of it. Done properly using tools that most people still haven't figured out. And that gap between the people who know this and the people who don't, that's your edge [music] right now. So, today we're going to break this down into four stages. By the end of this, you'll have a complete AI system for landing your dream job from the first application all the way to the offer letter. Before we begin, I wanted to tell you a little something about myself. I run three AI companies and train people across 150 plus countries. I've studied AI thoroughly and now to teach you how to leverage it and become successful. In fact, my goal is to teach AI [music] to 1 billion people across the globe. In order to do this, apart from making videos, I also share the latest AI updates, prompts, tools, and a lot of tips and [music] tricks in my free WhatsApp community called Staying Ahead. If you'd like to be a part of it, you'll find the link in the description. Let's get into it. Okay, so let's start at the beginning. Most people search for jobs like this. They go to LinkedIn, they type marketing intern, they filter by location, and they scroll, and they apply to whatever looks right. Here's the smarter way to do this. There are AI assistants now, tools like Claude, Perplexity, or certain features within ChatGPT that can actually read a webpage. They're called agentic [music] tools, and yes, it may sound complex, but stay with me. Here's what you do. Step one, open your LinkedIn profile, make sure it's updated. This is my profile. Step two, open an AI assistant. How do we do this? So, Perplexity AI has this tool called the Comet browser. It can browse the web on your behalf, interact with pages like a human would, understand context from what's on screen, and complete tasks across sites. Let's use it for this task. All we have to do is type Comet browser on Google, tap on this link, and download it. It's that simple. Once it installs, set up your profile, and you're ready to have a conversation with it. Step three, this is the prompt I'm using, and I want you to copy this type of prompt because the specificity is what makes it work. Here is my LinkedIn profile. Please review my complete profile, my experience, skills, and background, [music] and find me suitable jobs in San Francisco or the Bay Area that I could apply for. Justify why the top five jobs are the best fit for me, and why I should apply for them. These are the jobs I must apply for overall. Also, provide a note on the percentage fitment of each job profile relative to my current profile. That is [music] information you can act on. And here's what we get, a summary on my LinkedIn profile, then the five jobs I should apply for, and a fitment score for each. This tells me I have EdTech [music] domain expertise, AI product experience, and I'd be a good fit for head of revenue in an EdTech A

hook_text_english: Claude Code just killed video editing. Premiere Pro is now optional.
full_text_english: Claude Code just killed video editing. 🤯 I'm not exaggerating.The job that used to require Premiere Pro, Final Cut, hours of manual cutting, and a video editor on retainer can now be done by a single AI workflow.I tried it this week. It blew my mind.Here's the part nobody is talking about. Video editing used to live in a different universe from AI.You couldn't ask ChatGPT to cut a video. You couldn't ask Claude to add subtitles.The work happened in heavy desktop software with steep learning curves.That just changed.Claude Code, paired with an open source video tool called Remotion, can now edit any type of video for you.Add split screens. Generate subtitles. Cut and structure your entire video automatically.The setup takes 3 steps and about 10 minutes.→ Install Claude Code from the official site by pasting one command into your terminal→ Add the Remotion skill to Claude Code by pasting their setup→ Open Claude Code, run the Remotion skill, and describe what you want your video to look likeThat's it. The AI handles the cutting, the layout, the subtitles, the structure. You describe the outcome. It builds the video.Here's why this matters more than the average AI tool drop.Video has been one of the last creative industries that AI couldn't touch end to end.You could generate clips. You could write scripts. You could clone voices.But the actual editing, the part that decides whether a video lands or flops, was still a human bottleneck.That bottleneck just dissolved.If you're a creator, this changes how fast you can ship.If you're a brand, this changes your content cost structure. AI isn't going to replace Leaders; Leaders who build AI systems are going to replace Leaders who don't. The PROMPTS framework isn't a tech manual—it is a blueprint for personal and professional sovereignty.

Awesome framework, Vaibhav! The *PROMPT* method—_Pick your toolkit_, _Refine_, _Open your world to AI_—is a game‑changer for anyone looking to actually leverage AI in everyday business. Loved the tip to focus on a tight stack of 5–6 tools instead of juggling dozens.👉 How have you seen the “Refine” step (tuning temperature & tokens) impact real‑world results for your clients or projects? 🚀

This really changed the way I look at AI.Most people are still treating AI like a tool to “try.”But the real shift happens when you start building systems around it instead of using it randomly.“Skill → System → Business” was probably the strongest part for me.

"Synthesize" is where most people quit too early. The shift from "6 different tools" to "a team of agents that work together" is what separates people who use AI from people who build with AI. What's the first orchestration setup you'd recommend for someone just starting out?

This is one of the most practical frameworks I’ve seen for approaching AI adoption Vaibhav Sisinty👏🚀A lot of people are still treating AI like a tool they occasionally use, while the real shift happens when AI becomes part of your workflow, systems, and decision-making process.Also loved the idea of becoming an “AI Generalist.”The future probably belongs to people who can combine domain expertise + AI leverage + problem-solving across functions instead of relying on just one technical skill.And this line stands out strongly:“Skill. System. Business.”That’s the real evolution AI is enabling — turning individual capability into scalable output 💯Also yes, a detailed YouTube breakdown of the PROMPTS framework would be super valuable 🔥

The 'S' for Synthesize is where the real ROI lies. Moving from 'using tools' to 'orchestrating teams of AI agents' is the shift I'm seeing in my own work. When you stop doing AI tasks and start letting AI run your pipelines, that’s when you truly scale. Great to see this being shared at IIM Ahmedabad.

Strong framework.Most people are currently stuck at the “tool discovery” stage — trying hundreds of AI apps without integrating them into actual workflows.The real value starts when AI moves from:assistance → automation → orchestration.That’s where productivity compounds.The most important point here is probably “Synthesize.”Using one AI tool is easy.Building connected systems where multiple AI agents handle research, content, operations, follow-ups, reporting, and execution together — that is where businesses will create disproportionate advantage.In the next few years, companies may not simply hire based on specialization alone.They will value people who can:• understand business problems• work across functions• use AI to increase output at scaleThat combination will define the next generation of high-leverage professionals and founders.Very relevant perspective for students and working professionals both.

Vaibhav Sisinty This really connected with me.When I started building solutions related to my own domain using AI, I realized we don’t always need deep coding knowledge to start building something meaningful.We just need clarity on the problem and the idea — AI helps us bring it to life 🚀

I'm stuck between P and R.How do you actually finalise your 5-6 without spending months testing each one? Is there a decision filter, like this tool wins for writing, this one for research, this one for automation or does the stack depend so heavily on the person's work type that there's no universal answer?And on the Refine step - temperature settings, max tokens, model differences - where does someone who isn't technical actually start? Is there a non-jargon way to understand what these levers actually do in practice, not just in definition?Would vote for the full IIM A recording as-is. Frameworks land better when you hear the context and the questions behind them.
The biggest shift is when people stop treating AI like a tool and start treating it like infrastructure. That’s where output really compounds.
As an IIM Ranchi MBA going deep into AI, this resonates hard. The B-school crowd has the strategy chops; what most lack is shipping discipline. Sessions like yours close that gap fast.

Spot on, Vaibhav! From a systemic perspective, the PROMPTS framework is the ultimate blueprint for protecting our Cognitive Bandwidth. When we shift from manually executing tasks to orchestrating AI agents, we drastically reduce our daily Systemic Friction. This transition from "tool user" to "system builder" effectively lowers our Allostatic Load, allowing the "Human Hardware" to focus purely on high-level synthesis rather than exhausting background processing. The future belongs to those who successfully automate their friction!

One thing I’m realising while building BWS is that the world is changing faster than traditional learning models can keep up with.Curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to learn continuously are becoming far more valuable than just having information. Vaibhav Sisinty
The biggest shift is not people using AI.It’s people learning how to turn their thinking into systems through AI.That’s where the real leverage starts compounding. Vaibhav Sisinty

We’re entering a phase where the gap between two people won’t be intelligence.It’ll be orchestration.One person using AI occasionally vs another building an entire cognitive operating system around it.Btw I agree with many others like me here. The raw recording hits completely different!

Powerful perspective, Vaibhav Sisinty! The distinction between 'knowing AI' and 'converting skill into a system' is what will separate the 10M solo-preneurs from the rest. Most people are stuck at the 'Pick your toolkit' phase; seeing a path toward 'Pipeline' and 'Synthesize' is eye-opening. I’d vote for the YouTube deep-dive to see the 'how' behind the framework!

Co-founded TriggerlyAI after several years inside large retail ops, building voice agents and automations for service businesses. The "Skill. System. Business." line is the same arc I see playing out with our clients now. The owner who wins is never the best operator in the business, it is the one who builds the system that runs the business when they are not in it. AI just collapsed the time it takes to build that system from years to weeks.
The people who benefit most from AI will probably be the ones who learn how to integrate it into daily workflows instead of treating it like a separate tool.

I am sure the energy in that room was electric. As someone deep in the AI and software space, I would love to know what was the one question from the IIM-A students regarding the future of tech that surprised you the most? Keep inspiring!
The shift from using AI to orchestrating AI is where the real leverage gap between people will open up.

AI Generalist isn’t just a role, it’s a survival skill in the current tech shift.The faster you adapt, the more relevant you stay. Vaibhav Sisinty

caption: Claude Code just killed video editing. 🤯 I'm not exaggerating.The job that used to require Premiere Pro, Final Cut, hours of manual cutting, and a video editor on retainer can now be done by a single AI workflow.I tried it this week. It blew my mind.Here's the part nobody is talking about. Video editing used to live in a different universe from AI.You couldn't ask ChatGPT to cut a video. You couldn't ask Claude to add subtitles.The work happened in heavy desktop software with steep learning curves.That just changed.Claude Code, paired with an open source video tool called Remotion, can now edit any type of video for you.Add split screens. Generate subtitles. Cut and structure your entire video automatically.The setup takes 3 steps and about 10 minutes.→ Install Claude Code from the official site by pasting one command into your terminal→ Add the Remotion skill to Claude Code by pasting their setup→ Open Claude Code, run the Remotion skill, and describe what you want your video to look likeThat's it. The AI handles the cutting, the layout, the subtitles, the structure. You describe the outcome. It builds the video.Here's why this matters more than the average AI tool drop.Video has been one of the last creative industries that AI couldn't touch end to end.You could generate clips. You could write scripts. You could clone voices.But the actual editing, the part that decides whether a video lands or flops, was still a human bottleneck.That bottleneck just dissolved.If you're a creator, this changes how fast you can ship.If you're a brand, this changes your content cost structure. If you're an agency, the model that pays for your team just got rewritten in a week.We always assumed video editing needed complex software and hours of manual work. Now it can be done inside a single conversation with an AI.The next decade of content won't be won by who can afford the best editor.It'll be won by whoever describes their video clearly enough.I share insights like this every day in my free WhatsApp community.Join here ↓https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ
