hook_text_english: ChatGPT now lives inside my Slack, Gmail and Calendar. And it's doing work without me even being online.
full_text_english: ChatGPT now lives inside my Slack, Gmail and Calendar.And it's doing work without me even being online 🤯 OpenAI just shipped Workspace Agents last week.It's not a tab you open. Not a chatbot you ping.It sits there. Reads your emails. Watches your calendar. Acts.I built two for my own agent this weekend.Both working through tasks while I was sleeping Full breakdown of both agents I built 👇 https://lnkd.in/dKx4tKdf
body_summary_english: Announcement of OpenAI's new Workspace Agents feature with creator's example of two custom agents built for autonomous task completion. Includes link to full technical breakdown.
comments: This is where it starts to get interesting - when AI stops being a tool you "use" and starts quietly running inside your workflow. The real unlock will be when teams figure out what *not* to automate so context and judgment don’t get lost. Vaibhav Sisinty How are you handling guardrails or data access for these agents?

This feels like a real shift from using AI to actually delegating work to it. The biggest change isn’t efficiency, it’s autonomy, where tasks move forward even when you’re not actively involved. If this scales well, the real advantage will go to people who design systems, not just execute tasks. Vaibhav Sisinty
The shift from AI that answers to AI that acts changes the entire value model - the bottleneck is no longer intelligence, it's trust and workflow design.

This shift to autonomous AI agents truly feels like a leap into the future of work! It's exciting to see AI moving beyond just a tool we use to a proactive teammate managing workflows seamlessly. The focus on workflow design and trust is so critical—automation without context can easily backfire. Looking forward to seeing how these agents evolve, especially the potential for ready-made, subscription-based models that make integrating AI accessible and hassle-free for teams of all sizes. Great insights, Vaibhav and everyone.

Vaibhav Sisinty this is where things get really interesting—AI is shifting from responding to executing 🤯 The real leverage now isn’t just knowing how to use tools, it’s knowing what to delegate to them. People who figure out how to offload repetitive workflows early will move 10x faster than everyone still doing everything manually 💯

We are witnessing the "Evolution of the GPT." First, they were chatbots; then they were specialized GPTs; now, they are autonomous Workspace Agents. The next 12 months will likely see these agents moving from just "reading and watching" to actually "negotiating" between different company workspaces.
This shifts AI from a tool to a teammate. The real edge now isn’t using AI—it’s designing the right workflows and guardrails so it can act autonomously without creating noise or risk.
The interesting part isn’t that AI can do tasks while we sleep.It’s that work is increasingly becoming something we architect… not just execute.
The shift from “tools you open” to “systems that run in the background” is massive. It changes AI from something you use to something that actually manages workflows.

Have you considered making your tested agents available on a rental model? A day's trial with limited tokens, a clear prerequisites checklist, a step-by-step setup guide post-payment, and privacy assurances built in. Someone like me doesn't need to build an agent from scratch. I need one that's already been stress-tested, does a specific job well, and can be onboarded in under an hour. If the agent delivers even 20% of what you're showing here, a monthly subscription is an easy yes.
cool until it takes an irreversible action on stale context, oversight design matters way more than the automation itself...
This is a solid step forward. But execution will determine how well it scales.

hook_text_english: The crazy part of 500K in 6 months is how we made it happen 😅
full_text_english: The crazy part of 500K in 6 months is how we made it happen 😅Full breakdown 👇6 months ago, we decided to start YouTube to teach AI in a much deeper way.Instagram was great, but 3-minute reels were not enough.The biggest blocker?My time.I travel like crazy and running a 200+ people team is already a full-time job.So we built an AI avatar of me.The team syncs with me for 2-3 hours every week, my thoughts and ideas get baked into the content, but I don’t have to physically shoot every video.The big question was:Will people actually watch 20-minute long-form videos made with an AI avatar?Today, we crossed 500K subscribers.Probably one of the only YouTube channels in the world to cross 500K subs where 100% of the content is AI-generated using my avatar.Not everyone loves the avatar part, but millions of people are learning AI through it.That’s what matters.Next goal: 1M subs in the next few months.And then, the biggest AI education channel in the world.We’re just getting started 🚀

The teams that win won’t be the ones using more AI, they’ll be the ones integrating it smarter.Would love to explore how this evolves into product strategy and real-world execution.

I just loved this ♥️
Wowww, Let's have it 🙂‍↔️
Not copilots anymore… these are operators.

A human builds an AI avatar to teach humans about AI. 😂I have a wild idea: What if I 'steal' this entire strategy, but shoot all the videos with actual human effort and emotion?Who is ready to support this 'crime'? 🙋‍♂️ If you want to see a real Human vs. AI showdown, you are just one click away. Let me know below!
Vaibhav Sisinty depends on if you’re serving consumers or producers

Everyone’s worried about AI replacing creators, but you have used it to augment the creator. This is a huge shift for personal branding—scaling the face without losing the brain. Can't wait to see this hit 1M.

You have one avatar that broadcasts. What if you had multiple avatars that taught - each one specialised in a specific AI tool, available on demand, with real-time tests built into every session?Learner picks a tool. Avatar teaches. Mid-session quiz. Fail? Repeat the module. Pass? Move forward. No cohorts. No waiting. No scheduling.The difference between a video and this model is accountability. Right now people watch and drift. With live testing baked in, they have to engage to progress.Someone who wants to learn Claude workflows at midnight doesn't need a webinar - they need a patient, infinitely available tutor who won't let them skip the hard parts.You've already built the avatar. The testing layer and the specialisation are the only things between here and the most scalable AI school in the world.
Leverage isn’t just doing more, it’s designing systems where your thinking scales without your time. Vaibhav Sisinty
Traditional content:* Founder-dependent* Time-heavy* Limited outputAI-led content:* System-driven* Time-leveraged* Scalable outputLess effort per video.More impact per system.

Vaibhav Sisinty this is a perfect example of leverage done right—most people think growth is limited by their time, but here the system scales the output without scaling the effort 🤯 The real takeaway isn’t just the AI avatar, it’s the clarity on what matters: your thinking, your ideas, your consistency. The format can evolve, but if the value is strong, people will stay. Smart execution > traditional methods 💯
Amol George this comment was made with AI smh

This is a fascinating example of how AI is reshaping content creation—not just as a tool, but as a scalable way to share knowledge while still keeping the creator’s thinking at the center. The real win here is impact: if more people are learning and adapting, the format becomes secondary. 🚀

While the growth is undeniable, the real challenge isn't the 1M sub mark—it's the "trust equity" long-term. Avatars are great for information transfer, but for high-stakes leadership, people still crave the imperfections of a live human. The "hybrid" model (AI for tutorials, Human for deep-dives) might be the actual end-game for most founders.
Scaling to 1M will test if the avatar can carry nuanced Q&A sessions. Long-form education often lives or dies on audience interaction, not just delivery.

The 500K is impressive,but the actual breakthrough here is the workflow behind it separating the founder's thinking from the founder's presence. Most creators treat those as the same thing. You've proven they're not. That's the insight that'll age well regardless of what happens to avatar tech.

Vaibhav Sisinty Interesting execution, removing the founder bottleneck without losing strategic input. Scale happens when systems carry your thinking, not your time. The real win here is not the avatar, it’s the leverage it creates.
Honestly the AI avatar play is wild. Most creators run out of hours before ideas. Curious how retention looks vs your manual videos tho, that's the real test.

We saw major ai news, including China's new free AI that outperformed Claude and ChatGPT on the toughest tests, alongside Anthropic's free AI integration. Elon Musk's Grok also introduced capabilities not found in any other AI, while OpenAI made significant ai updates with their smartest ai model ever.

This week was the craziest week in AI all of 2026. China just shipped a free AI that beat Claude and ChatGPT, OpenAI completely reborn itself with 3 massive announcements, and one image AI became #1 in the world overnight. Plus 13 more updates including a Vercel breach, a guy who decoded his own DNA at home, and SpaceX's $60 billion AI deal. At the end, I am breaking down the new ChatGPT Images 2.0 with 5 use cases you can steal today.

Also, it costs a fraction of what Claude or GPT charge. Its new model has a few features worth discussing today. One, it can code for over 12 hours straight across 4,000 plus tool calls. Three, up from its previous limit of 100 sub aents, now 300 AI workers can run simultaneously. This means that 300 specialists now tackle different parts of your project at the same time. One prompt can produce finished websites, slide decks, spreadsheets, and documents. It's clear the West no longer has sole ownership over this AI race. OpenAI just officially launched GPT 5.5 along with a stronger pro version and they are calling it the smartest and most intuitive model they have ever shipped. And look, I have already done a full deep dive on this on the channel where I gave Codeex one prompt before bed and it built me a working app for the MacBook while I slept. [music] The big shift is that it now plans steps on its own, checks its own work, and finishes multi-art jobs without you having to handhold it through every move. Here are some top use cases. One, you can ask it to make you an app like the Space Mission One, and it will do it for you. Second, it can do the same thing with 3D games and look at the results. It's extraordinary. Finally, if you give it an existing spreadsheet and ask it to modify it after conducting a financial analysis and it will figure it out and do it for you. It is live right now in chat GPT and codeex for plus pro business and enterprise users. API access is coming soon. Hackers have spared noon. Even AI can be breached now. Few days ago, one of the biggest platforms on the planet was breached. Meet Versel. You have probably never heard of them, but you use them every day. They are the invisible engine behind OpenAI's website, Nike, Walmart, and millions of other sites on the internet. It's so big that it's valued at $9.3 billion. Yet, they got breached. Here is the crazy part. The hackers did not attack Versel directly. They attacked a tiny AI productivity tool that one Versil employee had installed. transcript: A Chinese founder just upgraded the AI that's beating [music] OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic. It's still free, open- source, and now delivers finished files. Welcome to day 23 of AI update of the day. Remember Kimmy? [music] The Chinese AI that could split itself into 100 agents [clears throat] running at the same time. It just upgraded and the jump is genuinely unreal. The old Kimmy gave you chat responses. The new Kimmy gives you real files, PDFs, [music] data sets, presentations, finished documents you download and use. Let me make this real for you. You are job [music] hunting. You have 100 companies on your list. You upload all 100 job descriptions into [music] Kimmy. One prompt. Kimmy spins up 300 agents. Each one reads a different job, analyses the requirements, tailor your CV specifically for that role. You get back 100 finished CVs formatted, ready to [music] send. A human would take weeks to do that. Kimmy does it in one run. But here is where it gets wild. You upload 40 research [music] PDFs, one prompt. Kimmy reads all 40, cross references them, and delivers a full literature review with a cited data set. That is PhD level research from [music] one click. That is the shift. The old version answered your questions. The new version does your job everything at once. This is not an AI assistant anymore. This is a department. And the part that should worry every expensive AI company in the west. Kimmy costs a fraction of what [music] chat GPT and Claude charge. Open source practically free and the output is now bigger than what most paid tools can deliver. The AI race is no longer about who is smartest. It is about who ships the most work. And right now that answer is not coming from Silicon Valley. I share updates like these in my WhatsApp community.

transcript: This guy just landed ahead of AI role by vibe coding and AI system that evaluated 700 jobs for him and automated his job hunt. The crazy part that he made it open source so that anyone can use it for free. Here's what happened. A guy named Santiago wanted a job. So instead of doing what we all do, he opened Claude code and said, "Build me a system that does my entire job search for me." And Claude built it. You give it a job link. Claude reads the whole post in, scores how good the fit is, rewrites your resume specifically for that role with the right keywords so the company's software doesn't reject it, and turns it into a ready to send PDF. By the way, this is crazy AI update of the day, and today is day 16. Now, with the tool he coded, he didn't use it for 10 jobs or 50, 740 plus. That's how many jobs this system evaluated. Over a 100 custom RS generated, each one built from scratch. It scans 45 plus companies on its own. Anthropic, OpenAI, Stripe finds openings, checks the fit, fills out application forms, even gives you word for word scripts for when they lowball your salary. One guy, no team, no recruiter, just him and Claude code. And if you go to the project page right now, Claude is not listed as a tool. It's listed as a co-creator right next to his name. So what happened when he actually used it? He didn't just get a job. He landed ahead of applied AI role and then he did something nobody expected. He made the entire system open-source, 100% free. That means anyone watching this right now can use his exact system to hunt for job. 7,000 people have already grabbed it in 2 days. If you know someone who's looking for a job right now, send them this reel. Comment job and I will send you the link to my WhatsApp community where I have shared the full setup step by step in a document and also shared the link to the repo. It's free.

hook_text_english: GPT 5.5 just launched. This is not a smarter chatbot.
full_text_english: GPT 5.5 just launched. 🤯 And this is not a smarter chatbot. It is a completely different class of AI.Until yesterday, every AI worked the same way. You ask. It replies. You ask again. It replies again.GPT 5.5 does not work like that.You give it a goal. It opens tools. Browses the web. Writes code. Creates documents. Checks its own work. And keeps going until the entire job is done.Smarter than GPT 5.4. Just as fast. Uses fewer tokens. All at the same time.Here is the thing ↓ I built an entire Mac app with it.One prompt. It wrote the code, debugged the errors, fixed them, and shipped it. I did not touch the keyboard. It worked autonomously for 8 hours straight.This is not an assistant you talk to. This is a coworker that finishes the job.And the timing is brutal.Everyone thought Claude had the lead. OpenAI was "falling behind." Sam Altman was being counted out.GPT 5.5 just closed the gap. In one drop.The real question is not which AI is smarter anymore. It is who lets AI execute first.Whoever ships first, wins.I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ
body_summary_english: Explains GPT 5.5's autonomous agent capabilities that work toward goals rather than reactive chat. Creator shares personal experience building a Mac app with one prompt.
comments: This is where AI gets truly interesting—not as a single tool, but as infrastructure behind a workflow.The real shift happens when isolated tasks become connected systems.

This is a powerful leap, Vaibhav Sisinty. Centralizing every layer of social data and letting one AI orchestrate the entire workflow isn’t just a productivity boost — it’s a structural advantage. When creation, insights, and decision‑making sit in one unified system, the quality of output and speed of execution change completely.Excited to see how this evolves and what new possibilities it unlocks for creators and growth teams.

Centralising all social media data with one AI orchestrating the whole system is exactly the kind of tool every growth marketer has been wishing existed. This changes how you make content decisions — no more gut, just signal.

Impressive build, Vaibhav Sisinty centralizing all social data with AI feels like a big step toward safer, more efficient workflows. Tools like this can really help reduce the invisible tax of context‑switching and let us focus on execution.
Vaibhav Sisinty nailed it—when you centralize your data, you stop guessing and start making smarter moves 📊 One system, one AI, full clarity… that’s how content becomes a real growth engine 🚀
This isn’t just a build. It’s the end of tab‑switching chaos. One AI, one system
Whoever centralizes their data and thinking like this will have an unfair advantage in content and growth 🚀 Vaibhav Sisinty
That’s seriously impressive—bringing everything into one AI-powered system is next level! Love the vision and execution—can’t wait to check out the full build in your video.

Love to know the comparison in detail.
Interesting
Om Salve
Amazing
This is 🔥
excelente es muy buena
That's amazing!
Nice vid Vaibhav 👍

This is exactly where most creators lose — not because they lack content, but because their data is scattered across 5 different tabs with zero clarity.One AI. One system. Full signal. 🔥Funny thing — I see the same problem with personal brands.They post everywhere but have no system to tell them:→ Which content is attracting clients→ Which profile words are converting→ Where exactly they're losing high-ticket opportunitiesThe ones centralizing their brand signal — just like this build — are the ones closing ₹50k-₹1L deals without chasing.Vaibhav, are you planning to add brand perception tracking to this? Would love to see how positioning data feeds into content decisions. 👇
Not another AI tool — this is a system

Super exciting direction for AI agents. The shift from “chatbot that answers” to “coworker that executes end‑to‑end” is exactly what will change how we build products and run businesses.Curious to see how teams adapt their workflows and guardrails when an AI can autonomously write code, ship features, and iterate for hours. Execution-first AI really might become the new competitive edge.
The interesting part is not autonomy itself, it’s how much oversight you need per hour of execution. That ratio decides if this scales or not.
Codex
I think GPT-5.5 isn’t an upgrade. It’s a mindset shift — from prompting tools to delegating outcomes. The gap between early adopters and everyone else just got wider.

The shift from AI as an assistant to AI as a coworker is the part that changes everything. Most people are still using AI like a faster search engine. The ones treating it like an autonomous agent are getting completely different results. What have you noticed in terms of adoption speed?
Most users won’t notice hype-level changes, but workflows will get faster and sharper.Feels like AI is quietly moving from impressive to actually indispensable

Vaibhav Sisinty Interesting shift. The real unlock isn’t smarter responses, it’s autonomous execution. When AI starts completing workflows end-to-end, the leverage for founders changes completely. The winners won’t be who uses AI, but who builds systems around it fastest.

GPT 5.5 as a coworker rather than an assistant is a game-changer for productivity. For FP&A specifically, imagine AI autonomously pulling data from multiple ERPs, reconciling, building dashboards, and flagging anomalies — all while you focus on the story behind the numbers.
The autonomy angle is powerful, but I think the real differentiator will be how reliably these systems execute in real-world constraints. Execution + trust = adoption.

The real question isn't GPT-5.5 vs Claude. It's: which AI can you trust with a task that has real consequences — a financial model, a legal document, a customer-facing product? That answer depends on your use case, not on launch day hype.

The shift from conversational AI to autonomous execution is what changes the game. Most people are still treating AI as a tool they need to guide step by step. The ones who'll see real productivity gains are the ones who learn to frame complete outcomes instead of individual tasks. That's a different skill set entirely — it's less about knowing the right prompt and more about knowing the right objective.
The way GPT 5.5 handled building your entire Mac app, including debugging, really blurs the line between tool and teammate. I'm curious how this shift will affect developer roles moving forward.
This is a big change: going from answering to actually doing things changes how people use AI in a big way.In practice, the real test will be how reliable and easy to understand those outputs are.

transcript: So, Chat GPT just did something way better than what Claude has done so far. They put AI agents [music] directly inside your workplace, and I do not mean another chatbot you have to open up and prompt. I mean actual agents that sit inside your [music] Slack, your Gmail, your calendar, and work alongside your team like a co-worker you just hired. You tag them, they do real work. Now, Claude has had managed agents for a while, [music] and they are genuinely powerful, but they are not as autonomous, not as easy to use, not as simple to actually live with day-to-day. Setting them up takes real effort. Chat GPT just made the whole thing dramatically easier. A 10-year-old could genuinely build one of these in 5 minutes. I spent the last 24 hours building two of them for my own business, and in this video, [music] I'm going to show you everything. You're going to see exactly how I built an AI that runs on Slack, made it work for my whole team in one click, watched it figure things out on its own, handed it a design project, and got proper output back. This is the video you want to watch before your co-workers do. Let's get into it. Hi, I'm Vaibhav. [music] I run three AI companies profitably, and my goal is to teach AI to 1 billion people across the globe. I share tools, the latest AI updates, and a lot of tips and tricks in my free WhatsApp community called Staying Ahead. If you'd like to be a part of it, you'll find the link in the description. All right, let's just build one because reading about Chat GPT agents is one thing, but actually watching it work is a whole different thing. So, here's what we're going to do. Let's open Chat GPT right now, set up our first agent from scratch, and in about 5 minutes, you're going to watch this thing do real work on my actual account. Let's go. So, let me just open up Chat GPT here, and there is a new feature I really want to walk you through. If you look at your left sidebar on Chat GPT, right at the bottom, you will see a new option called agents. And if you haven't noticed it before, that is because this is actually brand new. In the left sidebar of that panel, you will see a plus button that says create agent. Go ahead and click that, and this is the screen where every new agent starts. Now, there are two ways to build an agent here. First one, you can just describe what you want in plain English, like literally type build me an agent that does X, and Chat GPT will build it for you. Second one, and honestly, this is where most people should actually start, you pick one of the pre-built templates. So, let's go ahead and click on start from a template, and look at this. There are over 20 pre-built agents to choose from. You've got Chief of Staff, Sales Assistant, Data Analyst, Customer Reply Drafter, and a whole bunch more. Honestly, these templates are the fastest way to figure out what agents can actually do for you. For this walk-through, we are going to pick Chief of Staff, because if we can just automate figuring out what the hell we are supposed to be doing today, that is already worth the entire setup. So, click on Chief of Staff, and then hit use template. Now, it is going to ask you which apps you want to connect. You can see it is asking for calendar, chat, and email. For the calendar, you get options like Google Calendar, Microsoft, or something called Custom MCP. And this MCP option is what actually makes this thing powerful. You can literally connect any tool that has an MCP connector. So, things like Notion, Linear, or even your own CRM. And for anyone who doesn't know what CRM is, that is basically the software that businesses use to organize all their customer data in one place. Anyway, for this example, we are just going to keep it simple and use Google Calendar, Slack, and Gmail. So, let's go ahead and connect them, confirm, and create the agent. Now, watch what happens here. The second we hit create, Chat GPT actually sends itself a prompt, which basically says build me

hook_text_english: SpaceX just bought right to acquire Cursor for 60 billion dollars
full_text_english: SpaceX just bought the right to acquire Cursor for 60 billion dollars.Cursor had a problem every founder secretly fears. every company selling them the tools to run their business was also trying to kill them. OpenAI. Anthropic. Google. their landlords were also their rivals.then Elon walked in with a massive GPU cluster and an escape hatch.here's what actually happened↓Cursor is one of the fastest growing software companies ever. zero to roughly 2 billion dollars in annualized revenue in about 3 years.to keep growing, they needed to train their own AI. but training AI needs massive computing power. and the only companies that have that power are the same ones building Cursor's biggest competitors.OpenAI has Codex. Anthropic has Claude Code. Google has Gemini CLI.Cursor was paying its rivals rent while those rivals tried to replace them.then SpaceX made the offer nobody saw coming.Elon's company has been building one of the biggest AI compute clusters on earth. they just handed a massive chunk of it to Cursor.in return, SpaceX got two things.one. the right to buy Cursor for 60 billion dollars one year from now. roughly 15% above Cursor's current valuation.two. if SpaceX walks away and never buys Cursor. they still pay 10 billion dollars just for the partnership.read that again. 10 billion dollars just for walking away.that is more than Cursor was valued at a year ago.here's what nobody is saying.this was never a straight acquisition. it's a compute deal dressed up as one.SpaceX didn't need a code editor. Cursor didn't need money. they traded the one thing they each had in excess. compute for Cursor. distribution and product for SpaceX.and Elon just bought a one-year call option on the future of coding for 10 billion dollars.that's the deal.
body_summary_english: Cursor, a fast-growing software company, was acquired by SpaceX to escape vendor lock-in from AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, enabling independent AI training capabilities.
comments: speed isn’t the same as quality or differentiation.If everyone uses the same tool, outputs start looking the same.The real edge is still taste, positioning, and refinement

Old workflow:* Hire designer* Wait days* Iterate slowlyNew workflow:* Prompt the system* Generate instantly* Iterate in minutesMissing layer:* Clear thinking* Strong narrative* Real validationDesign is no longer the constraint… storytelling is.
What’s interesting is not how fast this gets done… but how it changes where the real work sits.If the output is instant, then the thinking behind it becomes the differentiator.
Speed is impressive, but taste and strategy will still separate great brands from average ones.
If design systems can be generated this fast, execution speed becomes the real competitive advantage now

The “landlord is also your competitor” problem is real. I’ve seen companies grow fast… then hit a ceiling because their core dependency sits with a rival. This kind of deal isn’t about ownership. It’s about control over the one constraint that actually matters.

This is a fascinating strategic move. SpaceX securing compute independence while giving Cursor an escape from Big Tech dependency is a masterclass in vertical integration. The $10B walk-away clause is the real genius here.

Interesting move. Feels like they’re betting more on where developers spend time than just the tech itself. Curious to see if it actually changes how people build or just stays a strategic play on paper.

WOW !Interesting structure—this isn’t a typical M&A move, it’s a strategic compute hedge.Cursor’s core risk was always dependency on competitors for infrastructure. This deal effectively removes that bottleneck while giving SpaceX asymmetric upside—a call option on one of the fastest-growing AI-native products.The $10B “walkaway” clause is the real signal here. It prices compute access as a strategic asset, not just a cost line. We’re moving into a phase where control over infrastructure is as goor as product innovation.Net: Cursor gets independence to scale,SpaceX gets optionality on the application layer. Smart trade on both sides.

The landlord-rival dynamic is exactly what most people missed 🔥 Cursor wasn't just buying compute; they were buying freedom from a conflict of interest that was getting worse every quarter. And Elon wrote a $10B cheque to stay in the room. Real question nobody's asking, though, what happens to Cursor's neutral platform positioning once Grok is baked in?
You mentioned SpaceX committed $10 billion even if they walk away-do you think this kind of “walk-away fee” changes how startups negotiate with giants like OpenAI or Google?

Wild how this isn’t really an acquisition—it’s Cursor buying independence from its own competitors.Paying your rivals for compute while they build your replacement was never sustainable. This deal flips that power dynamic completely.
I think similar to Lovable , startups like Cursor are paying rent to LLM model companies who are in turn creating competing products 🙈 Difficult to be a niche player in this space haha nowadays

Cursor was valued at $9B a year ago, $29B five months ago, $50B this week in funding talks, and $60B in a potential acquisition. That's a 7× valuation increase in 12 months 🤯This makes it the most expensive AI acquisition so far, if this deal happens.
Vaibhav Sisinty This shows where AI is heading—tight integration between tooling and compute. The real advantage will come from owning both.
Whether this deal is real or not, the takeaway is clear: in AI, your biggest dependency is often your biggest threat.

The part that stands out to me is the strategic de‑risking on both sides:Cursor gets neutral, massive GPU access, so it can keep compounding without feeding rival models.SpaceX turns excess compute into a high‑upside product+distribution play, with a clearly priced walk‑away clause.Feels like a blueprint for how fast‑growing AI-native products will negotiate with infra giants going forward...

transcript: I'm going to come clean about something. I'm not a designer, never was. In fact, in fifth grade, my art teacher told me I could forget about becoming an artist. She was not wrong. I still can't draw a circle that closes properly. But watch this. I made all of this just today morning. My art teacher would lose her mind. Except this isn't skill, it's a tool, and not [music] just any tool. This is the one that quietly shook the design world last week, even hit Figma's stock. It's called Claude design. But here's the twist. Claude design isn't the real breakthrough. The real leap is the model behind it, Opus 4.7. For the first time, AI can actually see, reason, and create visual work properly, and that changes everything. Because now a founder who can't afford a designer suddenly has something that feels like a full creative team. [music] And there's one trick inside this that nobody's talking about. I'm going to show you everything. Let's get into it. Quick thing before we go ahead, everything you're about to see, the exact prompts, the brand files, the outputs, all of it is already inside my WhatsApp community. It's free. Link is in the description. You can grab it and follow along step-by-step. >> [music] >> Claude design is web-only right now, not available on your app yet. If you open up the left sidebar and scroll all the way down, you will see design. Click it and you're in. When you click create design system, it asks you to upload your brand assets, your fonts, your logo, your GitHub code, your existing landing page. But what if you don't have a brand and are starting from zero, building something completely new? Here is where most people get stuck, but I'll show you how to build this from scratch. Say we want to build this Indian health watch brand. We have the product idea, but zero fonts, colors, or vibe. Step one, pick two brands whose aesthetic you already love. I picked Apple Watch because of that clean premium feel, and Ultrahuman because of that energetic health vibe. I want a combination of these two. Step two, open a website called firecrawl.dev. It's free to sign up. This is a tool that scrapes any website and turns it into structured data. Go to playground. Paste Apple Watch's URL to scrape and hit run. Just remember to select the feature to extract the branding. That pulls out only the colors, fonts, and design language, nothing else. Now copy that output as JSON. Do exactly the same thing for Ultrahuman. Second JSON copied. Now the magic, open a fresh Claude chat, paste both JSON, >> [music] >> and give it one prompt. I am trying to build a brand focused on health monitoring watches, which is very sleek and premium, tailored to the Indian market. I have given two brands that I have admired. Create and synthesize a new brand language, which feels like an amalgamation of the two. >> [music] >> And what comes out is genuinely wild. Look at this. Claude gives you a core brand positioning, a design philosophy, a full color palette with exact hex codes, typography strategy, voice and tone guidelines. It literally generates a complete brand identity, stitched together from the two references you already love, tailored for your specific audience. And because it's created something new, nobody else can copy you. The output is unique every time. If you were building your brand today, which two brands would you want Claude to mash together? Drop them in the comments. Genuinely curious what combinations people pick. Now, to understand why this is such a big deal, you have to zoom out a little and look at what this new tool is actually trying to disrupt. Because this isn't just about design, this is about one of the most painful tools in business history. PowerPoint. Every MBA uses it, every consultant, every person in business. Billions of hours are spent every year just fighting with charts, aligning boxes, picking fonts that don't look completely awful. It's probably the single most used and the single most painful to

hook_text_english: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is live. For free. Indian marketers haven't noticed.
full_text_english: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is live. For free.And Indian marketers haven't noticed what just hit them. 🤯The generator era of AI images is over.Every old model had the same problem. Garbled text. Broken layouts. Hindi? Forget it.Here's what ChatGPT Images 2.0 can actually do:→ Renders Hindi and Bengali properly. For the first time ever inside an AI image.→ Browses the web mid-generation. Ask for an OpenAI merch poster, it pulls the actual products from the actual website.→ Builds slides, infographics, posters, UI screens. Not art. Design assets.→ Ratios from 3:1 wide to 1:3 tall. Built for LinkedIn banners and Instagram stories without cropping.→ 2K quality. Free tier included. Thinking mode for Plus, Pro, Business.The old models were brushes.This one is a designer that happens to use pixels.If you run a brand in India paying for 4 different design tools. Btw, time to rethink that stack.Thoughts?I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ
body_summary_english: Technical breakdown of ChatGPT Images 2.0 capabilities including Hindi/Bengali rendering, web browsing, design asset generation, and variable aspect ratios. Awareness-focused.
comments: Big shift—but people are underestimating where the impact is.This isn’t about better images. It’s about collapsing the entire content workflow into one loop.Design tools won’t disappear—but they move from creation to refinement.
The Hindi rendering alone kills half the reason brands outsource static ad creative here. Curious how it handles brand color locks at volume.

Hindi renders properly in an AI image and that’s somehow buried in the middle of the post. That deserved the headline. 😀Four design tools replaced by one prompt. Indian marketers aren’t sleeping on this — they just haven’t opened the tab yet.
It's preety good 👍
This is huge. Finally, AI images that actually understand local languages and design needs. Game-changer for marketers in India and beyond! 🎨🔥 Vaibhav Sisinty
Just saw the notification in my Chatgpt Account, its cool and really awsome. 1 Subscription is enough for image and text both.

Vaibhav Sisinty sir, I see you post, and I understand why the world is excited. It's a massive leap in generative AI.But here is a contrarian perspective. The real disruption for a business or a creator isn't just about generating "studio quality" images for free. The hardest problem this new capability introduces is actually how to protect your brand's identity and the integrity of your creative assets in a world where anyone can generate anything that looks like it was made by you.At Credenta, with our nine patents, we are solving a similar kind of problem for physical assets, ensuring structural integrity. But for your domain of digital growth, the next billion-dollar question is not about the quality of the generated output, but about establishing an immutable, verifiable chain of custody for AI-generated assets. How do you prove what was originally yours? How do you manage version control when every iteration is instant?I'd love to hear your take on this IP and authenticity challenge. In your experience, what is the biggest blind spot for creators when they start using these powerful tools at scale?
Wow..... Exciting to see this 🎉
Fantastic

hook_text_english: Every week I think AI has peaked. Every week I'm wrong.
full_text_english: Every week I think AI has peaked. Every week I'm wrong.This week was the worst.Here's what dropped 👇→ Dario Amodei said 50% of entry-level jobs could be gone in 5 years. He's the guy building the AI doing it.→ Google made NEET and JEE mock tests completely free on Gemini. Coaching centers charge lakhs for this.→ OpenAI's new Codex controls your entire Mac. Connects to 111 apps. Watches your workflow while you sleep.→ Anthropic dropped Claude Design. Describe what you want, it builds full websites, decks, and brand videos. Canva should be worried.And that's not even half of it.Claude Opus 4.7 is live. Grok voice APIs are 10x cheaper than ElevenLabs. A robot ran a half-marathon in 50 minutes.I broke all of it down in this week's video. What launched. What it means. What you should actually do about it.If you're not paying attention to AI right now, AI is still paying attention to you.Watch the full video 👉 https://lnkd.in/gpyXinCs
body_summary_english: A comprehensive roundup of major AI announcements including job displacement predictions, free educational tools from Google, Mac automation via Codex, and Claude Design capabilities.
comments: Big shift—but people are underestimating where the impact is.This isn’t about better images. It’s about collapsing the entire content workflow into one loop.Design tools won’t disappear—but they move from creation to refinement.
The Hindi rendering alone kills half the reason brands outsource static ad creative here. Curious how it handles brand color locks at volume.

Hindi renders properly in an AI image and that’s somehow buried in the middle of the post. That deserved the headline. 😀Four design tools replaced by one prompt. Indian marketers aren’t sleeping on this — they just haven’t opened the tab yet.
It's preety good 👍
This is huge. Finally, AI images that actually understand local languages and design needs. Game-changer for marketers in India and beyond! 🎨🔥 Vaibhav Sisinty
Just saw the notification in my Chatgpt Account, its cool and really awsome. 1 Subscription is enough for image and text both.

Vaibhav Sisinty sir, I see you post, and I understand why the world is excited. It's a massive leap in generative AI.But here is a contrarian perspective. The real disruption for a business or a creator isn't just about generating "studio quality" images for free. The hardest problem this new capability introduces is actually how to protect your brand's identity and the integrity of your creative assets in a world where anyone can generate anything that looks like it was made by you.At Credenta, with our nine patents, we are solving a similar kind of problem for physical assets, ensuring structural integrity. But for your domain of digital growth, the next billion-dollar question is not about the quality of the generated output, but about establishing an immutable, verifiable chain of custody for AI-generated assets. How do you prove what was originally yours? How do you manage version control when every iteration is instant?I'd love to hear your take on this IP and authenticity challenge. In your experience, what is the biggest blind spot for creators when they start using these powerful tools at scale?
Wow..... Exciting to see this 🎉
Fantastic

As a 2nd year CS student, this hits differently. By the time I graduate, the job market will look nothing like it does today. Honestly? That’s terrifying and exciting at the same time.The way I see it : the students who’ll survive this wave aren’t the ones who just learn tools. They’re the ones who learn how to build WITH AI faster than anyone else.So that’s the bet I’m making on myself right now.

The jarring part isn't that half of entry-level jobs disappear—it's that the transition happens in 5 years, not 50. Most organizations have a 10-year strategic plan. By the time they're done planning, the jobs that were supposed to exist are already gone. Speed of change outpaces speed of response.
The real threat isn’t AI itself it’s professionals treating AI like news instead of a skill. In every industry, the gap between AI users and non-users is about to become career-defining.

Hard to keep up at this point, Vaibhav Sisinty 😅Feels like every week resets the benchmark of what’s possible. The real edge now isn’t knowing everything—it’s knowing how to adapt fast and actually use these tools.

→ Google made NEET and JEE mock tests completely free on Gemini. Coaching centres charge lakhs for this. So hyped up. 1st of all, nobody is charging that much. Secondly, the mock within these AIs is hardly of much value to the students. I would say there's a bit of fearmongering and overhyping stuff here, not undermining the power of AI though.

The pace is actually insane. 🤯 These tools are shifting the focus from 'execution' to 'strategy' faster than anyone expected. If you aren't integrating this tech into your workflow now, you're already behind. Great recap, Vaibhav!
The pace isn't slowing down, it's compounding, and that's the part most people aren't ready for.
“Every week I think AI has peaked. Every week I’m wrong.” — most accurate sentence written in 2026. 😀A robot ran a half-marathon and that’s somehow the least alarming thing on this list.
This post really resonates with me, it's a reminder of the power of community and collaboration. Vaibhav Sisinty
And every week you are packed with ai 😅

AI is bound to be part of our every day life. It's a powerful tool to increase our productivity and redefine our lives. People who will work with it will survive. Rest will struggle.

hook_text_english: AMD Developer showed blueprint for India's AI future at India AI Summit
full_text_english: hashtag#Sponsored_by_AMD India doesn't just want to use AI, it wants to own its AI future. And AMD Developer just showed up with the blueprint. At the India AI Summit, AMD brought something called Helios. An all-in-one AI supercomputer, but instead of filling up a building, it fits into server racks. Most powerful processors, latest AI chips, high-speed networking, full software layer, all working together out of the box. A single rack doing what only the world's top supercomputers could a few years ago. But here's the part that actually matters. It's completely open. No vendor lock-in. No proprietary walls. No one company controlling your stack. PyTorch, TensorFlow, your own tools - all plug in natively. Why does this matter for India? Because India is done renting AI from other countries' clouds. The goal is sovereign AI infrastructure, AI power that India owns, controls, and runs on its own soil. And this isn't a promise. It's proven. AMD already powers the world's most powerful supercomputers - Frontier, El Capitan in the U.S., and Arka and Arunika right here in India. Open infrastructure isn't a feature. It's the foundation of an entire country's AI ambition. If you want to learn more about AMD Developer AI solutions from Cloud to Datacenter to Consumer devices for your AI needs go to 3 And if you're someone who builds with AI or wants to start go check out the AMD AI Developer Program. It's free, it gives you access to AMD's tools and community, and it's built for builders at every level. Link in the pinned comment!
body_summary_english: AMD presented Helios, an all-in-one AI supercomputer fitting into server racks with open architecture and no vendor lock-in, representing India's path to AI independence.

Vaibhav Sisinty What's happening now isn't a peak—it's just a ripple before the tsunami.You're right that every week seems to set a new ceiling for what's possible. But what if our entire framework of "peaks" is misleading? Morgan Stanley predicts a truly transformative AI breakthrough in the first half of 2026—a leap so substantial that most organizations aren't prepared for it. Meanwhile, experts like Satya Nadella argue that 2026 will be the pivotal year when AI moves from standalone models to integrated systems designed for real-world impact.We're not approaching a plateau; we're at the base of a new S-curve. The Stanford AI Index 2026 reveals that AI agents handling real-world tasks have already jumped from a 20% success rate to 77%. And here's the truly unorthodox part: perhaps the most disruptive AI won't be the one that replaces jobs, but the one that creates entirely new forms of intelligence we can't yet name. The real question isn't whether AI is peaking—it's whether our collective imagination is keeping pace with what's actually coming.
Here's the link: https://bit.ly/41XDEo6
Vaibhav Sisinty Ads are ignored. Stories get shared. If your ‘sponsored’ post doesn’t feel native, it’s just noise.

What stands out is the architecture narrative—integrated systems like Helios signal a push toward modular, open AI infrastructure where compute, networking, and software layers are bundled but not locked, enabling more control over deployment and governance at scale. Vaibhav Sisinty
The real shift isn’t just smaller supercomputers—it’s moving from dependency to sovereignty. Open stacks + local infra is how countries turn AI from a service into a strategic asset.

Big shift if this plays out, Vaibhav Sisinty 🚀Owning the infrastructure layer changes everything—cost, control, and long-term innovation. If India gets this right, it’s not just about using AI, it’s about shaping it 💯

This highlights an important trend in the global AI ecosystem. As AI adoption grows, more countries are starting to focus not only on using AI but also on building and controlling their own AI infrastructure. Open systems like this could play a big role in enabling local innovation and giving developers more flexibility to build. Vaibhav Sisinty

This is an interesting shift in how countries are thinking about AI. It’s no longer just about using AI tools,  it’s about owning the infrastructure behind them. The idea of sovereign AI infrastructure will likely become a major priority for many countries in the coming years.

Really happy to see some contributions in AI from India. I was waiting for something like this from India. I would definitely love to see what AMD's supercomputer does. I know it will be great when Vaibhav Sisinty collaborates with them. Really excited to know.

hook_text_english: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO
full_text_english: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO 🤯An engineer named John Ternus takes over on September 1, 2026.And here is the part nobody is talking about.Apple has had only 3 CEOs in the last 30 years. And each one tells you exactly what Apple thought was broken.→ Late 1990s. Apple was dying. Bad products, broken strategy. They brought back Jobs a product guy to fix what you hold in your hands.→ 2011. Apple had the best products on the planet. But needed massive scale. They picked Cook an operator to build the greatest supply chain in history.→ 2026. Apple has the best devices on earth. But the weakest AI story in big tech. They just picked Ternus a hardware engineer.See the pattern?Apple doesn't pick CEOs based on who's loudest or most famous.Apple picks CEOs based on what the next era demands.And this pick tells you exactly what Apple thinks the AI war will be won with.Not bigger data centres. Not bigger models. The chip in your phone.Ternus has been inside Apple's hardware lab for 25 years. He killed Intel. He shipped Apple Silicon. He quietly built every iPhone, iPad, Mac, and AirPod you have ever owned.While OpenAI, Google, and Meta burn billions on cloud AI, Apple just said: the next war is on-device. And we have the one guy who has been training for this for 25 years.Btw, Tim Cook added 3.6 trillion dollars to Apple in 15 years. That's nearly 700 million dollars of market cap every single day he was on the job. Probably the best operator in tech history. Not kidding.He is not leaving because he failed. He is leaving because the era he was built for is ending.Might be exactly the right guy at exactly the right time.Or the biggest gamble in Apple's history.
body_summary_english: Tim Cook is being replaced by engineer John Ternus in September 2026. The post analyzes how Apple's three CEOs in 30 years reflected different strategic priorities.

apple doesn’t pick the best ceo, it picks the right problem to solve next.if they’re betting on hardware for ai, it means they think distribution will beat raw intelligence.
Madhav Pangarkar you are right about their approach, but picking up CEO means picking the suitable leader that aligns with the vision.

Interesting perspective on leadership transitions. Apple’s pattern clearly shows they optimize for the next bottleneck, not past success. If the shift is toward on-device AI, then hardware + silicon becomes the real battleground. The real question is: can Apple translate its hardware advantage into a compelling AI ecosystem fast enough? That’s where the game will be won or lost. 🚀

Vaibhav Sisinty. This is a great example of how leadership decisions reflect broader business priorities, not just succession. Whether accurate or not, it highlights a useful lesson: companies hire leaders based on the problem they’re trying to solve.
Internflare well said

Interesting take, Vaibhav Sisinty.Apple’s pattern is actually very clear when you zoom out—each CEO isn’t just a leader, they’re a solution to the next bottleneck.If this bet on on-device AI works, it won’t just be a strategy shift… it’ll redefine how we think about AI itself.Big move. Let’s see if it pays off.

This succession feels less like a leadership change and more like a strategic signal. Cook was the operator Apple needed for scale. Ternus looks like the bet Apple is making on hardware + on-device AI.Interesting part is: Apple may not win the AI narrative first, but if it controls the device layer, it can still control how people actually experience AI.

Great breakdown. Leadership isn’t about legacy, it’s about timing. From Steve Jobs to Tim Cook to John Ternus—each choice reflects Apple’s biggest gap at that moment. The real question now: can hardware-led AI beat cloud-first models?
Apple Silicon was a vertical integration win, but AI isn’t just chips. It’s data, distribution, and iteration speed. That’s where Apple has been slower compared to others.
Interesting way to look at leadership transitions. It’s less about replacing a person and more about aligning leadership with the company’s next strategic challenge.
Leadership shifts aren’t always that perfectly aligned with one “problem.”Still, if the bet is on on-device AI, this move definitely signals intent.
This is a powerful way to look at leadership transitions. Apple isn’t changing CEOs, it’s changing direction. From products → to scale → to on-device intelligence. The pattern is very clear.
Interesting take on Apple’s leadership shift. It’s clear they’re focusing on hardware‑centric AI, and that’s a bold move. Wishing the team success as they tackle the next frontier.

title: India’s AI future won’t be rented, it’ll be built.
caption:
#Sponsored_by_AMD | India’s AI future won’t be rented, it’ll be built. 

AMD Helios brings sovereign, open AI infrastructure to India. No ecosystem lock-in. No gatekeepers. 

Just the same proven tech behind the world's fastest supercomputers, now built for Indian builders and startups. 

Join the free AMD AI Developer Program. Link is in the pinned comment.

--------

To Know More,
Follow Vaibhav Sisinty On ⤵︎

Instagram @VaibhavSisinty
https://www.instagram.com/vaibhavsisinty

Twitter @VaibhavSisinty
https://twitter.com/VaibhavSisinty

Facebook @VaibhavSisinty
https://www.facebook.com/vaibhavsisinty/

LinkedIn - Vaibhav Sisinty
https://www.linkedin.com/in/vaibhavsisinty
transcript: India doesn't just want to use AI, it wants to own its AI future and AMD just showed up with the blueprint. I'm excited to partner with AMD. So, here's what happened. At the India AI Summit, AMD brought something called Helios. Think of it as an all-in-one AI supercomput, but instead of filling up an entire building, it fits into server racks. What's inside? AMD packed in their most powerful processors, the latest AI chips, high-speed networking, and a full software layer. And the performance is wild. But here's the part that actually matters. It's completely open. That means no company gets to lock you in. You're not stuck with one vendor's software, one vendor's tools, one vendor's rules. Developers can use the frameworks they already know and partners can plug in their own tech wherever they want. Now, why does this matter for India? Because India is done just renting AI from other count's clouds. The goal is to build sovereign AI infrastructure, natural disasters, and advancing healthcare to helping the next generation of Indian startups compete on a global stage. And AMD's tech already runs the world's most powerful supercomputers, Frontier, El Capitan in the US and many others in Europe. So, this isn't a promise, it's proven. If you want to learn more about AMD AI solutions from cloud to data center to consumer devices for your AI needs, go to amd.com/ai. And if you're someone who builds with AI or wants to start, go check out the AMD AI developer program. It's free. It gives you access to AMD's tools and community. And it's built for builders at every level. Links in the description. This video is sponsored by AMD.

hook_text_english: The godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.
full_text_english: I don't know how to not think about this anymore.the godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.Geoffrey Hinton isn't saying AI will take your job. He's saying AI might take jobs as a concept.For 10,000 years, every tech revolution followed the same script. A machine killed one type of work, a new type appeared somewhere else.Tractors killed farm jobs. Factories opened. Factories closed. Offices opened. Offices got automated. Knowledge work opened.Every time one door closed, another one opened behind it. That's the only reason the economy kept working.Hinton's point is that AI isn't opening a new door. It's already standing behind every door you could possibly run to.Call center worker loses their job. Retrains as a coder. AI replaces the coder. Pivots to law. AI replaces law. Tries writing. AI replaces writing.There's no lane left to switch into.Goldman Sachs already estimates two-thirds of jobs in the US and Europe are exposed to AI. Entry-level hiring in AI-exposed fields is down 13% since ChatGPT launched. Job openings overall are down 30%.Humans spent 10,000 years breaking through limitations. Food scarcity. Distance. Physical labor. Every time we hit a ceiling, a bigger economy appeared above it.Hinton is saying human intelligence was the last ceiling. And maybe he's right.But here's what's also true. Every person reading this has access to tools today that didn't exist 3 years ago.The gap between someone who uses AI well and someone who doesn't is now wider than any degree ever was.That gap is the new door.Walk through it.vc: Milk Road AI
body_summary_english: Geoffrey Hinton warns AI could eliminate jobs as a concept, breaking historical cycle where automation creates new opportunities. Explores implications of AI-driven displacement without replacement job creation.
comments: If you ask any AI—whether it’s ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok, or Perplexity AI—who talks the most about AI replacing jobs, the answer often points to none other than Vaibhav Sisinty.@ Vaibhav Sisinty, have you heard of the Jevons Paradox? Understanding it might give a different perspective and help reduce unnecessary fear around AI and jobs.

As a BCA student, this hits differently. I'm literally studying to enter the same job market Hinton is talking about. But I've stopped seeing AI as competition and started treating it as the skill gap itself. The students who graduate knowing how to build with AI won't just survive this shift — they'll define it.
The takeaway isn’t job loss—it’s capability shift. The edge now comes from how well you leverage AI, not what role you hold.

Powerful perspective by Vaibhav Sisinty🤯The fear isn’t completely wrong—but it’s incomplete. AI might compress roles, but it also expands leverage. One person today can do what a team of 5–10 used to do.The real shift isn’t “jobs disappearing,” it’s “average skill becoming obsolete.” People who think, adapt, and use AI strategically will still stay valuable.The game is no longer about what you know, it’s about how fast you can learn and apply. 🚀
While past revolutions created new physical or knowledge sectors, this one demands an entirely new baseline of technological fluency. Embracing these tools is no longer optional—it's survival.
The last line about the gap is closer to what’s visible day to day. Teams aren’t replacing everyone, they’re relying heavily on the few who can integrate these tools into actual workflows.
There’s fear in this, but also clarity. Vaibhav Sisinty Maybe the shift isn’t about finding a new lane anymore—it’s about learning how to run faster with AI in every lane.
Important perspective. AI may compress roles, but it expands leverage.The shift isn’t finding a new lane. It’s learning how to operate at a completely different level.
The ability to hold attention has become one of the most valuable skills today. Content that stays with people creates far more impact than content that simply gets views.
Vaibhav Sisinty Powerful perspective. AI may compress traditional job pathways, but it’s also expanding leverage for those who learn to use it effectively.
Every new update from OpenAI, Anthropic or Google gives me the gut feeling that another profession is going to go.

It should go. Human shouldn't do any job. I strongly believe it won't happen though completely as it seems. You are selling course on AI you should scare more people and tell the extreme things which i respect every entrepreneur should do that actually..

hook_text_english: Elon and I are basically best friends now, I guess.
full_text_english: Elon and I are basically best friends now, I guess 🤣Okay not really. But something happened yesterday that's worth talking about.I was on an important call.My phone wouldn't stop buzzing. WhatsApp, Slack all going off at the same time.I genuinely thought something had gone wrong. Server down. Family emergency. Something serious.I opened it and realised what had actually happened:Elon had retweeted my tweet.Half my group chats thought I'd died. The other half thought I'd finally made it.But the retweet isn't the interesting part.The interesting part is what it revealed about how the internet actually works in 2026.One retweet from the right account outperforms a month of consistent posting. Not by a little. By an order of magnitude.That's the game now.And here's what nobody wants to admit:Twitter keeps getting called toxic. Over and over. People keep saying it's dying.But there is no other platform on earth where every top founder, CEO, VC, and world leader is actively doom scrolling at the same time.Not LinkedIn. Not Instagram. Not even close.One post. One reshare from the right person. And suddenly you're in front of everyone who matters.No wonder Elon paid $44 billion to buy it.He wasn't buying a social network.He was buying the last room on the internet where real power still hangs out.
body_summary_english: Humorously recounts receiving Elon retweet and reactions from contacts. Reflects on how internet attention works and the nature of viral validation in social media.

Stop scrolling guys! Before reading this post, can someone tell me how many times Vaibhav has used the word 'AI' in the last 24 hours? Will he get a prize? 😂"
Yash Tank hmm, if there was a prize, then maybe I'd use AI to find the answer  😉 Vaibhav trains people to use AI. So there is a bias there.

This resonates deeply with what I see in my AI workshops. The fear is real, but the moment someone learns to "walk through the door" and co-create with the tool, the anxiety turns into agency. We aren't just losing jobs; we are losing the "busy work" that we used to mistake for a career.

Speaking of HUMINT - it got my attention. And wrt the call centres, personal touch, human touch/ connection might be more appreciated and perhaps humans will start appreciating their fellow beings even more with OSINT in the equation. You can’t replace a heart and a nervous system. A soulful experience.
MONICA Malhotra to Vaibhav Sisinty Btw, just heard the video and said what I said after seeing GF of AI that I had to hear.
This isn’t surprising if you’ve been on X/Twitter long enough. Distribution there has always been step-function, not linear. You stay invisible until one node picks you up.

Interesting insight from Vaibhav Sisinty👀This really shows that distribution > consistency in today’s attention economy. One signal from the right node can outperform months of effort.But it also highlights a deeper game—being in the right rooms (digital or physical) matters more than just creating content.Consistency builds the base, but leverage comes from visibility among the right people. 🚀

Crazy how one signal from the right node can outperform months of effort. Distribution isn’t equal, it’s concentrated. Makes you realize: consistency builds the base, but leverage comes from being seen by the right people at the right time. Vaibhav Sisinty
This is such an underrated point. Distribution still beats consistency when it comes to reach—but consistency is what gets you noticed by the right amplifier.
Vaibhav Sisinty Distribution today is less about consistency alone and more about proximity to leverage. One signal from the right node can outperform months of effort.
This is exactly what we’re seeing across the AI ecosystem—skill matters, but distribution multiplies it. Builders who understand both will win.

The “$44 billion for a doom-scrolling room” framing is genuinely the best way I’ve heard it explained. Everyone’s been asking why — and the answer has always been hiding in plain sight. CEOs, VCs, founders, world leaders — no other platform has that density of decision-makers in one feed. LinkedIn is résumés. Instagram is aesthetics. X is where actual leverage lives. Wild that a retweet can do what a month of content can’t. Proof that on X, access beats algorithm every time
I agree Twitter has that room, but I’ve also seen serious business happen on LinkedIn. It’s less loud, but more conversion-driven.

Grok entering voice APIs changes the whole competitive dynamic — STT + TTS in one package from xAI is a direct shot at ElevenLabs and Deepgram. the Elon retweet energy aside, this is actually a big technical move worth watching
Grok's voice API dropping right as everyone's building voice agents is not a coincidence. Do you think this kills third-party voice AI tools or actually expands the market for builders?

Vaibhav Sisinty, Funny thing is, people will still walk away from this thinking, “I need a viral post.” Reality is, you need one person with context + credibility to carry your words. Baaki sab noise hai.
Funny how one unexpected line can do more work than 10 well-written paragraphs. Attention really works in weird ways.

hook_text_english: Uber gave 5,000 engineers Claude Code in December. 😨 By April, CTO burned AI budget
full_text_english: Uber gave 5,000 engineers Claude Code in December. 😨By April, the CTO had burned through the entire annual AI budget.The tool didn't fail. That's the problem.When Uber goes all in on something they go ALL in.And that's exactly what happened with Claude Code.In December, 32% of engineers were using it.By February, that number was 63%.That is not a gradual rollout.That is a product so useful that engineers pulled it into their workflow faster than finance could model the spend.Here is the thing ↓ These engineers weren't building simple apps.They were working on rider-driver matching algorithms. Dynamic pricing engines. Real-time logistics across 70 countries.The kind of problems that burn entire context windows before you even get to the answer.The numbers are wild 🤯→ 95% of Uber's developers now use AI tools every single month→ 65% of all code written inside their IDEs is AI-generated→ 11% of all pull requests are now opened by agents. Not humans.→ AI costs at Uber are up 6x since 2024Read that last line again. 6x.And this is a company with a $3.4 billion R&D budget.The CTO literally said "I'm back to the drawing board, because the budget I thought I would need is blown away already."But here is what nobody is saying out loud.This is NOT a story about overspending.This is a story about a company discovering that its entire financial operating system was built for a different era.Traditional software has flat, predictable costs.You buy 5,000 seats. You pay the same bill every month.AI agents don't work like that.One engineer running Claude Code in an automated loop can drain a monthly budget in days.Multiply that across thousands of engineers. Running complex workflows. Simultaneously.The math compounds in ways no annual budget cycle was ever built to absorb.The AI race in 2025 was about which model was smarter.The AI race in 2026 is about who figures out the finance layer first.Uber won't be the last company to have this meeting.Your company might be next.I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

The budget-blown-in-four-months signal is actually a bullish one. When engineers pull a tool deep into their workflow that fast, the ROI case becomes obvious, the CFO just doesn't have the vocabulary for measuring it yet.
caption: Uber gave 5,000 engineers Claude Code in December. 😨By April, the CTO had burned through the entire annual AI budget.The tool didn't fail. That's the problem.When Uber goes all in on something they go ALL in.And that's exactly what happened with Claude Code.In December, 32% of engineers were using it.By February, that number was 63%.That is not a gradual rollout.That is a product so useful that engineers pulled it into their workflow faster than finance could model the spend.Here is the thing ↓ These engineers weren't building simple apps.They were working on rider-driver matching algorithms. Dynamic pricing engines. Real-time logistics across 70 countries.The kind of problems that burn entire context windows before you even get to the answer.The numbers are wild 🤯→ 95% of Uber's developers now use AI tools every single month→ 65% of all code written inside their IDEs is AI-generated→ 11% of all pull requests are now opened by agents. Not humans.→ AI costs at Uber are up 6x since 2024Read that last line again. 6x.And this is a company with a $3.4 billion R&D budget.The CTO literally said "I'm back to the drawing board, because the budget I thought I would need is blown away already."But here is what nobody is saying out loud.This is NOT a story about overspending.This is a story about a company discovering that its entire financial operating system was built for a different era.Traditional software has flat, predictable costs.You buy 5,000 seats. You pay the same bill every month.AI agents don't work like that.One engineer running Claude Code in an automated loop can drain a monthly budget in days.Multiply that across thousands of engineers. Running complex workflows. Simultaneously.The math compounds in ways no annual budget cycle was ever built to absorb.The AI race in 2025 was about which model was smarter.The AI race in 2026 is about who figures out the finance layer first.Uber won't be the last company to have this meeting.Your company might be next.I share updates like these in my free AI community on WhatsApp.Join here ↓ https://lnkd.in/gG2tUqkQ

This AI video discusses the concerning "AI layoff trap," where researchers suggest that AI replacing jobs will ultimately crash the economy. We examine recent oracle layoffs and major tech news, highlighting the significant ai impact on the future of work. The video delves into the economics behind these decisions, even as CEOs are aware of the potential economic downturn.
transcript: The richest people on earth are trapped. They know AI layoffs will crash the economy. They cannot stop firing workers anyway. Here is why. Welcome to day 22 of AI update of the day. Two researchers just proved AI layoffs will crash the entire economy and every CEO already knows it. The paper is called the AI layoff trap and it is already happening. Oracle fired 12,000 people in India. One email, 6:00 in the morning, job gone. Amazon, Meta, Atlassian, Block, all announced layoffs this year. All blamed AI. Here is the flowchart. Every company fires workers to cut costs. Every fired worker stops buying products. Revenue collapses across every sector. The companies that fired everyone go bankrupt. It is a prisoner's dilemma. And the math is insane. Two companies sell the same thing. Company A keeps its workers. Company B fires them and uses AI. Company B wins on price. Company A has to fire its people just to survive. Now multiply this by every industry on Earth. And this is the part that should scare you. It is not just workers who lose, the owners lose, too. The researchers proved even if you only cared about making rich people richer, this system still destroys more money than it creates. Every CEO can see the cliff. Every CEO keeps driving toward it because the first one to stop dies first. The AI trap on the economy is here. I share updates like these in my WhatsApp community. Link in bio.

transcript: Sam Alman just dropped an AI that can literally use your computer for you. And Anthropic is quietly becoming the next Apple of AI. Same playbook. Once you are in, you never need to leave. This week alone, they launched three major updates, including a tool that might just replace Canva entirely. And this thing is so powerful, it could threaten the entire design industry and every office job built around making PPTs, websites, or apps. Meanwhile, Google was quietly cooking up the biggest week of all. They came for Hani Singh and every music producer in the country. They came for every coaching institute in India teaching J and Neat. And they built an AI that literally puts you inside any YouTube video you watch. Talk about main character energy. Mark Zuckerberg is quietly building an AI clone of himself to take meetings when he cannot show up. And Perplexity just launched an AI that works entirely while you sleep. And that is just five of the 14 AI updates from this week. Hi, I am Vibhav. I run three companies on AI and train people on AI in over 150 countries. Every week I cover the AI updates from around the world that are actually worth your time. Plus, at the end, I will show you how I used Clawude Design to turn a simple screenshot into a full working landing page, a motion graphics brand video, and a full pitch deck without ever opening PowerPoint. While I make this video once a week, if you want AI updates in real time, I have a WhatsApp community which is free to join and I drop AI updates in time there. Link is in the description. Let us go. Anthropic just launched Claude Design and this is not a mockup tool. Watch what it does. You describe what you want, attach your files and Claude builds the full design in front of you. In this demo, someone typed, "Design an interactive globe showing how culture flows between cities, attached a spreadsheet and a document, and Claude built a full dark themed 3D rotating globe with glowing paths and live statistics. Then they typed add controls and sliders appeared that change the colors in real time. It works for everything. App prototypes, presentation slides, landing pages, dashboards. You can tweak fonts and colors with sliders. Leave comments on specific parts of the design and toggle dark mode all through conversation. And when the design is done, you export it as a PDF, a PowerPoint. Send it straight to Canva or hand it off to Claude code to make it real. Available now on all paid Claude plans. So, Anthropic just dropped a Claude Opus 4.7, a proper upgrade over the Claude 4.6 you have been using. And two things are genuinely different. First, Opus 4.7 sees images in three times higher resolution than 4.6. So, when you upload a screenshot, a photo, or a messy document. It catches the small stuff the old version used to miss. On Anthropic's own testing, image accuracy jumped from 57 to 79%. Second, it is way better at handling multi-step tasks, the kind where you ask Claude to do one thing after another in sequence. So if you tell Claude to research a topic, turn the findings into a table and share it. 4.7 finishes the whole thing with one/ird the errors 4.6 used to make. And it is live right now across every paid Claude plan. No upgrade required. But honestly, the most interesting launch from Anthropic this week isn't the model itself. Claude just launched something called routines. You give it a task once and Claude runs it on its own servers on your schedule even while your laptop is closed. Basically, Claude automates work for you while you are away. We actually made a full 15minute tutorial breaking down exactly how routines work and how to set them up. So, if you want the complete guide, the video is linked here. OpenAI just dropped the biggest Codeex update of the year. And this is one update that isn't just for developers. For anyone new to this, Codeex is OpenAI's AI work assistant that lives inside a desktop app. Up until now, it was mostly used by coders for writing

body_summary_english: Microsoft released VibeVoice, a free open-source tool that transcribes meetings and generates speech locally without subscriptions, disrupting existing voice AI SaaS businesses.
comments: If you ask any AI—whether it’s ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok, or Perplexity AI—who talks the most about AI replacing jobs, the answer often points to none other than Vaibhav Sisinty.@ Vaibhav Sisinty, have you heard of the Jevons Paradox? Understanding it might give a different perspective and help reduce unnecessary fear around AI and jobs.

As a BCA student, this hits differently. I'm literally studying to enter the same job market Hinton is talking about. But I've stopped seeing AI as competition and started treating it as the skill gap itself. The students who graduate knowing how to build with AI won't just survive this shift — they'll define it.
The takeaway isn’t job loss—it’s capability shift. The edge now comes from how well you leverage AI, not what role you hold.

Powerful perspective by Vaibhav Sisinty🤯The fear isn’t completely wrong—but it’s incomplete. AI might compress roles, but it also expands leverage. One person today can do what a team of 5–10 used to do.The real shift isn’t “jobs disappearing,” it’s “average skill becoming obsolete.” People who think, adapt, and use AI strategically will still stay valuable.The game is no longer about what you know, it’s about how fast you can learn and apply. 🚀
While past revolutions created new physical or knowledge sectors, this one demands an entirely new baseline of technological fluency. Embracing these tools is no longer optional—it's survival.
The last line about the gap is closer to what’s visible day to day. Teams aren’t replacing everyone, they’re relying heavily on the few who can integrate these tools into actual workflows.
There’s fear in this, but also clarity. Vaibhav Sisinty Maybe the shift isn’t about finding a new lane anymore—it’s about learning how to run faster with AI in every lane.
Important perspective. AI may compress roles, but it expands leverage.The shift isn’t finding a new lane. It’s learning how to operate at a completely different level.
The ability to hold attention has become one of the most valuable skills today. Content that stays with people creates far more impact than content that simply gets views.
Vaibhav Sisinty Powerful perspective. AI may compress traditional job pathways, but it’s also expanding leverage for those who learn to use it effectively.
Every new update from OpenAI, Anthropic or Google gives me the gut feeling that another profession is going to go.

This isn’t surprising if you’ve been on X/Twitter long enough. Distribution there has always been step-function, not linear. You stay invisible until one node picks you up.

Interesting insight from Vaibhav Sisinty👀This really shows that distribution > consistency in today’s attention economy. One signal from the right node can outperform months of effort.But it also highlights a deeper game—being in the right rooms (digital or physical) matters more than just creating content.Consistency builds the base, but leverage comes from visibility among the right people. 🚀

Crazy how one signal from the right node can outperform months of effort. Distribution isn’t equal, it’s concentrated. Makes you realize: consistency builds the base, but leverage comes from being seen by the right people at the right time. Vaibhav Sisinty
This is such an underrated point. Distribution still beats consistency when it comes to reach—but consistency is what gets you noticed by the right amplifier.
Vaibhav Sisinty Distribution today is less about consistency alone and more about proximity to leverage. One signal from the right node can outperform months of effort.
This is exactly what we’re seeing across the AI ecosystem—skill matters, but distribution multiplies it. Builders who understand both will win.

The “$44 billion for a doom-scrolling room” framing is genuinely the best way I’ve heard it explained. Everyone’s been asking why — and the answer has always been hiding in plain sight. CEOs, VCs, founders, world leaders — no other platform has that density of decision-makers in one feed. LinkedIn is résumés. Instagram is aesthetics. X is where actual leverage lives. Wild that a retweet can do what a month of content can’t. Proof that on X, access beats algorithm every time
I agree Twitter has that room, but I’ve also seen serious business happen on LinkedIn. It’s less loud, but more conversion-driven.

Grok entering voice APIs changes the whole competitive dynamic — STT + TTS in one package from xAI is a direct shot at ElevenLabs and Deepgram. the Elon retweet energy aside, this is actually a big technical move worth watching

One retweet can spike distribution.But consistency is what earns that retweet in the first place.
